entries.title: Digestion
entries.body:
Life in this unknown ocean shares its basic genetic code with Earth. DNA letters code for amino acid words, which are joined into strings like sentences to create proteins. Pioneers can also look forward to familiar delights such as sugar, starch, fat, and alcohol. 

However, this world's genetic code uses different words for amino acids, creating waxy and metallic proteins. Human stomach enzymes cannot digest them. Undigested mass builds up in the intestines, causing indigestion, intestinal blockage, starvation and gout.

This field adaptation allows your digestive tract to secrete alien enzymes built with human chemistry. Borrowing these enzymes bypasses the need to design a synthetic equivalent — allowing you to proceed with the mission. The chances of your digestive tract digesting itself are minimal.

entries.title: Heat Tolerance
entries.body:
Although your AEP suit can protect you against acute hot water burns for a limited time, prolonged exposure will cook your tissue like a sous vide pork tenderloin (simile flagged for review). Human proteins are sensitive to high temperature and will swiftly unravel.

Organisms that live near thermal vents can survive water above 65 degrees Celsius. Temperature may change from near 0C to above 100C in seconds. (Because pressure delays boiling, some deep hydrothermal vents can reach hundred of degrees C.) Pompeii worms, still extant in Earth's oceans, grow a coating or fleece of bacteria to survive exposure to hot water. These epsilonproteobacteria tolerate intense heat with heat shock proteins, which prevent and reverse the breakdown of cooked molecules.

Pioneer nanomachines will assist your body's cells in producing these heat shock proteins and monitor long term health effects. Regular return to safe water temperatures is advised.

Additionally, a layer of protective bacterial fleece between your skin and AEP suit will protect you from skin burns. Your PDA will monitor this bacterial culture and adjust its odor and secretions to prevent irritation. These bacteria have been derived from existing skin cultures and may help build appreciation for the role of healthy bacteria in day to day life. Say "thanks, staphylococcus."

entries.title: Pressure Tolerance
entries.body:
At 100 meters below the surface, the pressure on your body is over ten times what it is on land—like an elephant standing on every square inch of you. Humans must breathe air under equal pressure or the lungs cannot inflate, but this carries severe risks:

—Below 40m, the nitrogen in air becomes narcotic, causing impaired judgment, delirium, and anesthesia

—As pressure increases, oxygen becomes toxic, causing tunnel vision, tinnitus, vertigo, and convulsions

Each additional 10m of depth increases the pressure by 1 atmosphere. Breathing normal air at 600 meters and 60 atm of pressure is fatal. The extreme pressures force gas molecules into the body's tissues, saturating them like a soda and disrupting their necessary function.

Deep-sea organisms produce a molecule called TMAO (trimethylamine n-oxide). TMAO is a stiffener for water molecules, helping them hold their shape against pressure. Adapting your body to produce this stiffener will reduce the rate of gas saturation, allowing your pioneer nanomachines to clean up the fatal fizz as fast as it develops.This adaptation may result in long term liver damage, but the consequences fall beyond your current survival horizon. 

This adaptation will also prevent trimethylaminuria, a condition in which a seafood diet can lead to a powerful fishy body odor.

entries.title: Graffiti

entries.title: Incandescent Light
entries.body:
An incandescent light, created by electricity running through a resistant cable coated in a transparent insulating varnish. 

The depth of sediment coating suggests recent construction, within the past ten years. The pillar that mounts the light is much older, on the scale of centuries. 

This lightning technology is characteristic of the early Electronic Age, commensurate with Earth's 20th or 21st century.

entries.title: [PLACEHOLDER] Contact Lab
entries.body:
[PLACEHOLDER] This is an Axum site established to greet any Architects arriving on Proteus. (It was built by an Axum faction called the Soterists, who wanted to solve the climate collapse through alien intervention.)

It is meant to be VERY simple to build at EA1. Just a wall and some symbols. And if we can’t afford to get the symbols, it’s just a wall.

- Fiction
    
    The Axum discovered an Architect phase gate in orbit (well, actually, at the Lagrange point between Proteus and Apiary) and hoped they could signal the Architects to come save their planet from climate change.
    
    It was built to last for centuries, like the Crypter vaults.
    
    However, it was built by a different political faction than the Crypters—the Soterists.
    
    When the Architects arrived, the Axum intended for them to home in on signals from one of these labs. If the signals failed, the labs were built near sites of natural and cultural interest, like the Karakorum observatory and the Xanadu world tree. This lab wasn’t built right at the Xanadu world tree because the Crypters controlled that area.
    
    The labs were built underwater. There may be an above-water mast structure, if it has survived the centuries. We don’t need to build it now.
    
    The labs may be booby-trapped to destroy hostile aliens.
    

It is located in the Karakorum ‘farmland’ subregion, probably near a blight node.

entries.title: [PLACEHOLDER] Cymatic Pool
entries.body: [PLACEHOLDER] A pool of liquid metal. If you're tired of swimming in good old boring waters, you know what to do here.

entries.title: [PLACEHOLDER] Observatory
entries.body:
[PLACEHOLDER] The player spends all of Chapter 3 finding, powering up, and ultimately accessing this ancient Axum observatory and its attached Climate Lab.

Inside is a diorama of the Proteus solar system, as well as a star map which allows your PDA to determine your position in the galaxy. This will reveal that you are near the galactic core — further from Earth than any human has ever traveled, beyond any hope of communication or rescue.

Further up the Observatory is the Climate Lab, which reveals the recent history of Proteus, including the downfall of the Axum and their failed attempt to heal the Karakorum World Tree and stabilize this region’s ecosystem.

But you can’t get to any of this until you power up the observatory.

Success activates a diorama of the Proteus solar system, as well as a star map which allows your PDA to determine your position in the galaxy. This reveals that you are near the galactic core, further from Earth than any human has ever traveled and beyond any hope of communication or rescue.

The attached Climate Lab computer reveals the recent history of Proteus, the downfall of the Axum, and the failed attempt to heal the World Tree to stabilize this region’s ecosystem. It is THE big payoff for the EA1 story.

So what are we actually going to put here?

entries.title: [PLACEHOLDER] Observatory - Inner Sanctum
entries.body:
[PLACEHOLDER] The Observatory itself contains two setpieces.

The first is the **orrery and starmap.** This is a (stationary, for now) model of the Proteus star system. Around the orrery are tiny lights on wires, representing nearby stars. Scanning this orrery allows your PDA to determine your location in the galaxy. This may be immediately revealed to the player, or it may be concealed until a later story beat.

The second is the **telescope.** This is just a cool scannable. Maybe later we let players build their own; who knows?

The Observatory is connected to the Climate Lab. To quote earlier writing:

> Centuries later, as the Axum began to understand the damage their rampant use of natural gas had done to their climate, the Viceroys (a group of leaders appointed to manage the crisis) ordered the construction of a long-term climate lab here. The ancient Observatory made a perfect foundation: it had already proven it could survive the test of time.
> 

> The Climate Lab was built to last for centuries without maintenance. It’s a brutal, efficient, streamlined structure built of corrosion-resistant material. Maybe it’s made of teflon panels over a grid, like the Millennium Dome…
> 

> Whatever the case, the Climate Lab should cling to the Observatory below with a kind of desperation…It’s almost a weapon. Its purpose is to measure the collapse of Proteus’ climate and give the Crypters (Axum hiding in hibernation) a signal when it’s safe to emerge—or when things have gotten so bad that more extreme measures need to be taken.
> 

We do not have a good design for what happens inside the Climate Lab, but it should reveal things about the past and future of Proteus, and drive home that **this world is dying**.

To quote the design macro:

> Exploring further I enter what is clearly a newer part of the structure. Here there are many pieces of scannable alien technology. I learn about the alien computers, and the PDA infers that this was a lab built to study ongoing climate change. WIth the records it finds, it indicates that the planet has entered a late stage climate collapse, which the aliens were attempting to resolve. (NOTE: This assumes player has scanned axum language in the city. If not, they have to continue to find samples to learn the language enough to decode the message)
> 
> 
> It is unclear where the aliens have gone, but there seems to be evidence they've moved in near the world tree, a creature that apparently used to be numerous and anchored life on Proteus. They were working on a solution to heal the last world tree, but the society fell apart before they could finish.  Healing the roots and fighting the blight is only the first step of many if I want to save my new home, Proteus.
>

entries.title: Battery Terminal
entries.body:
A charging rack for personal batteries. Draws excess power from the habitat — will not function in a power shortage. Larger power cells used in submersibles and heavy equipment require their own specialized charger.

—Requires power input from base
—Load depleted batteries into charger; remove when fully charged (or early for emergency use)
—Vital for long term operation of tools and equipment

To avoid unpleasant surprises, check battery levels in your tools and return them to the charger before depletion.

"The Battery Terminal: it's not the end of the line."

entries.title: Biobed
entries.body:
The "Shanti Essentials" biobed is a ruggedized scanner/fabricator for human bodies.

Despite the power of modern scanners and computers, it's impossible to image and record every molecule in a human body as digital data. The biobed uses quantum imaging to store a 'ghost' of the patient in its optical lattice. This ghost can be printed back into matter by precise fabrication. When paired with a blackbox implant (which transmits the last brainstate before death back to its paired biobed on a phase channel), a biobed can detect a colonist's death and reprint them with no loss of memory or subjective experience.

—Does not require power or raw material (automatically collected from seawater)
—When synced with a biobed, colonists will be fabricated in a new body (a "print") if killed
—Blackbox implant allows transfer of final memories, but not all possessions
—Biobed quartz memory is stable into geological time
—Used to store Cicada colonists during long interstellar flight

Because the ghost is not digital data, it cannot be edited or duplicated. However, a biobed can record many ghosts during a single session and store them in quantum superposition. In theory, a single biobed could produce many duplicates of many people—populating an entire colony with clonal strains of a few initial colonists. This process is illegal under Trans-System Federation Law, and blocked at the software level in all legal biobeds.

entries.title: Biobed
entries.body: Creates a backup point for pioneers in case of covered work-related accident.

entries.title: Biolab
entries.body:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
The biolab is used to inject and remove biomods, temporary genetic tricks borrowed from native life.

The entry-level colonist can tolerate two biomods: one Active, one Passive. Interface with the biolab and choose one biomod for each slot. 

Active biomods must be triggered by the user, while Passive biomods activate by reflex when their conditions are met.

Biomods should be pre-tested and approved by colony medical staff. While a scanner could be modified to acquire new biomods from local species, the colonist would be liable for any resulting harm to self or others.

BACKGROUND
After the tragedies on 4546b and Rakshasa and the outbreak of kharaa in the Ariadne Arm, Alterra recognized that the human body was now the most vulnerable component of its deep-space strategy. Nobel Prize-winning research by Valenti and Pham produced the answer: a nanotech interface between human and alien biology. This interface (deployed in pioneer programs like the ARCHI suite) can reverse engineer alien gene products for safe expression by human cells.

Unlike full adaptations, these biomods never enter the colonist's genome: they are temporary and reversible. This helps would-be parents avoid awkward conversations about which biomods to inject for date night.

The Biolab is a colonist's one-stop point of service for biomods. Deep readers will note that it is also a triumph of human ingenuity over the pessimism of early anti-Expansion thinkers who believed that humanity's tight link to Earthly biota would make interstellar colonization impossible.

entries.title: Biolab
entries.body: Modifies your Adaptations.

entries.title: Biolab
entries.body: Modifies your Adaptations.

entries.title: Bioreactor
entries.body:
A bioreactor generates power from organic matter and local chemistry. The bioreactor's nanomachine culture (derived from the colonist's own body) decomposes organic matter into methane to burn with oxygen for power. Carbon dioxide and water emissions are captured for reuse as nanomachine mass.

With a safety waiver and some software skill, the bioreactor can produce chemicals like sugar or even explosives. These operations are best left to qualified experts.

—Large installation, requiring significant space
—Digests organic materials into power over time
—Accepts all biomatter, from raw pulp to whole living organisms (not vegetarian)
—Energy production is proportionate to the caloric density of the organic matter

*"The Bioreactor: it's green."*

entries.title: Bioreactor
entries.body: Rapidly decays organic matter and converts the biomaterial into power.

entries.title: Bulb Floor Lamp
entries.body: A crude attempt at form and function.

entries.title: Corridor
entries.body:
Titanium corridor. End user must supply a hatch and external power supply. If powered, generates a breathable atmosphere from seawater. Provides a basic field worksite or the first step towards a larger habitat. Shelters made entirely out of corridors have saved lives in disasters from deep sea to deep space.

—Supports small work facilities like the fabricator 
—Too small for larger facilities like the biobed or processor
—Excellent storage and rest location
—End user must supply hatch and power supply (such as solar panel)
—Can be extended in four directions

entries.title: Duplex Solar Panel
entries.body: Generates energy from sunlight. Reduced output at night or in deep water.

entries.title: Exterior Wall Light
entries.body:
Light is a critical resource for human work — and a dangerous industrial byproduct. Artificial light sources disrupt the natural behaviors of ocean life, attracting or driving away or native organisms. Early explorations of Earth's deep oceans were confounded by their own use of floodlights, which repelled the very life they were searching for. 

Expanding colonies should carefully monitor their light production and changes in the behavior of native organisms. The darker it is, the more dangerous a light can be.

entries.title: Fabricator
entries.body:
Fabrication technology is the power to rearrange matter at the molecular level. It catalyzed the great expansion from Earth, and remains the backbone of the modern world. 

Fabricators use coherent control lasers, mechanosynthesis and nanotechnology to position and bond atoms according to a blueprint. Blueprints are managed by FRM (fabrication rights management) cryptography. In emergency situations, fabricators unlock appropriate survival gear. New blueprints can be scanned in the field and added to the fabricator's library.

Despite their complexity, fabricators can be bootstrapped from simple feedstock like copper and titanium. Trace aluminum provides the sapphires for the fabricator's lasers. More specialized fabricators require more complex components.

Fabricators have replaced craftspeople in most industrial societies, allowing tailors, toolmakers, sculptors, welders, cooks, and other artisans to retrain into AI-assisted blueprint design.

Keep all body parts away from the fabricator while it operates. Despite common superstition, it is safe to think about the fabricator while it operates.

entries.title: Floor Locker
entries.body: A larger, more robust storage solution for wealthy pioneers. 30 Storage slots.

entries.title: Hatch
entries.body:
The "Super Barrique" selective passage portal allows for simultaneous two-way, multiple-crew traffic between a pressurized interior and a dangerous exterior. This eliminates the need for lengthy mechanical cycles which can interrupt traffic and reduce the joy of free motion.

—Heavy duty nanomembrane: molecular machines clasp hands across the entire surface of the hatch
—Sapphire laser systems provide energy and programming
—Membrane accepts crew members and cargo, while rejecting pressure and intruders
—Filters loose biomatter: great for kharaa monitoring

The membrane is only slightly weaker than solid titanium. In the event of a power loss, the nanomachines can sustain their grip for days. A mechanical iris is optionally provided to seal in the event of long-term power failure.

Instances of limb amputation or removal due to membrane error have been entirely eliminated from the design.

entries.title: Hydroelectric Turbine
entries.body:
Named for a legendary laundress, the "Nausicaa IV" hydroelectric turbine transforms moving water into power. Ocean currents turn blades, which spin the rotor of a polyphase generator to create electricity.

—Build inside a water current
—Connect to a nearby habitat or facility with power transmitter(s)

*"The hydroelectric turbine: a clever turn of phase."*

entries.title: Interior Door
entries.body: Bidirectional through-bulkhead indiscriminate ambulatory/atmospheric portal. Dual mode function (open and closed).

entries.title: Interior Wall
entries.body: A titanium partition. Dividing undersea habitats into watertight compartments is a proven survivability technique. Dividing living spaces into purpose-oriented compartments may help colonists focus on the task at hand and assist AI monitors in correctly coding behaviors.

entries.title: Keep Calm Poster
entries.body: Calm, rational behavior helps your AI advisors predict and pre-evaluate your future decisions. Do not take your circumstances personally. Remember that an effective system can subsume all critique into itself: humor can provide distance from your difficulties while improving your performance.

entries.title: Locker
entries.body:
The basic prefab locker (sold as the "Cachet" in the Alterra blueprint catalogue) comes default on all Alterra habitat builders. The Cachet is guaranteed to secure several kilograms of bulk cargo against flooding, depressurization, fire, and most animal intrusion.

Colonial records indicated that pioneers were wasting precious shift time sorting and labeling their lockers for quick access. PDAs now log the contents of every locker to a manifest, allowing fabricators to request materials directly from their storage point. No more sorting needed.

*"Alterra Lockers. Property rights are human rights."*

entries.title: Moonpool
entries.body:
An opening in the habitat's lower hull for direct entry by divers and submersibles.

Because the habitat's interior atmosphere is at the same pressure as the surrounding water, seawater cannot rise through the moonpool. No hatch membrane is therefore necessary, although this comes with sanitation and quarantine risks.

Moonpools are good locations for vehicle bays, high-traffic dive sites, and brief idylls such as kicking or stone-skipping. Be sure to wear your AEP suit to avoid loss of heat.

Caution: unless locked behind a bulkhead, moonpools offer free access to your habitat for intruders.

entries.title: NoA Terminal
entries.body:
This is a limb of the Noetic Advisor system. The NoA architecture consists of limb nodes (like octopus arms) governed by a minimal central executive, the Administrative Nucleus (ANu-NoA) aboard the Cicada's command module. If ANu-NoA is destroyed or cut off, the limb nodes will proceed with the mission to the best of their ability.

—Networks with other NoA nodes using shortwave radio, laser communication, or (if underwater) acoustic modem
—Fuses information from all habitat sensors and existing database to generate mission objectives
—Tracks pioneer work schedule and updates total debt
—If cut off from ANu-NoA aboard Cicada, will attempt to reconnect or rebuild a new ANu

The NoA system's behavior is well tested, and its core utility function is "genie safe", unable to create negative outcomes even with access to weakly godlike effectors. It is capable of extrapolating human intention and working proactively — "doing what you want, before you want it." NoA will always seek to maximize human welfare in accordance with human social norms, carrying the logic of transgov society to its end.

In order to remain weakly supervisable—reliably controllable by less intelligent systems such as pioneers—the Noetic Advisor cannot learn or alter its existing values or imperatives.

**IMPORTANT** Under normal operating conditions, the NoA should never interact directly with colonists. NoA is designed to use colonist PDAs as a frontend for human-facing communication. In emergencies, NoA can communicate via text terminal.  Remember that the Noetic Advisor does not accompany you into danger or enjoy an intimate relationship with your health and welfare. You must be your own advocate, as guided and informed by this PDA.

entries.title: Pioneer Hull Plate
entries.body: Hull plate commemorating a Cicada expedition sponsor.

entries.title: Power Capacitor
entries.body:
The IEEE 180-compliant "Fallback" power storage allows a habitat to bank power for use in the event of a shortage. Shortages may occur when the sun sets, fuel runs out, new systems are installed without proper planning, or even as a result of damage—but whatever the cause, the Fallback will keep the habitat operational until its own batteries are depleted.

—Automatically stores excess power
—Activates during a power shortfall; may deplete rapidly if the shortfall is large
—Adding more power storages increases the time the habitat can operate without power 

Power storage is one of the most dangerous elements of pioneer habitats. Practice safe habits: do not test unapproved tools on or near the power supply. Separate living quarters and work spaces from power storage to avoid catastrophic ruptures and thermal runaway of power storages or pioneers.

entries.title: Power Cell Terminal
entries.body:
A dual-purpose, heavy-duty power cell rack. Like the battery terminal, it recharges stored power cells with base power.

—Requires power input from base
—Load depleted cells into charger; remove when fully charged (or early for emergency use)
—Vital for operation of submersibles and other heavy equipment

Despite common superstition, fully charged cells can be left in the charger with no risk of detonation. 

Beware that chargers are a fire hazard. Do not immerse in seawater. Do not place body parts in charger.

"The Volta V: power on tap."

entries.title: Power Transmitter
entries.body:
The "Vaporway" wireless power pylon uses IEEE-compliant microwave beams to link power supplies to distant customers. Chaining multiple pylons together allows for effectively lossless power transmission across hundreds of meters. 

Underwater, the Vaporway exploits the Tamura-Murai capacitative coupling process to send current through ionized seawater.

—Build near power supplies or other power transmitters to extend their range
—Required to connect bases to detached generators, such as hydroelectric turbines

Avoid the clearly marked beam path. Do not spend prolonged periods in the beam. Despite the significant protection of your AEP suit, the results can be fatal.

entries.title: Processor
entries.body:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Construct in habitat room compartment. Select a desired process (such as titanium ingots from titanium), then load the input hopper and wait for the processor run to complete. Collect output from output tray. CAUTION: the processor's power draw increases sharply during a run.

BACKGROUND
The processor is a reverse fabricator, reducing complex inputs like salvage or biomass into simple raw materials. While standard fabricators can break down salvage, the processor is required to generate large pure outputs. The processor brings an arsenal of flash smelters, centrifuges, chemical washes, fractionators and magma electrolytic oxide cells to bear.

Refugee users may fondly recall political propaganda comparing Alterra to the sinister, controlling fabricator and the Mongolian Independent States to the robust, honest processor.

*"The Alterra bulk processor. Creative destruction."*

entries.title: Reaper Statue
entries.body: A statuette of the 4546b reaper leviathan, a predator made famous by Ryley Robinson's memoir 4546B: SURVIVING THE IMPOSSIBLE. As of last survey, flourishing in the ecologically renewed oceans of its homeworld. Sequenced and sampled for possible conservation transplant and use as a keystone species in terraformed oceans.

entries.title: Roof Antenna
entries.body: Dual-mode UHF/sonic antenna mast. (Radio nonfunctional when submerged.) Helps habitat AI systems communicate with remote beacons, drones and other habitats, although even with modern DOUBLE JANUS protocol, sonic modem bit-rate is limited.

entries.title: Roof Antenna Large
entries.body: "Earle IX" sensillum. Named by Alterra Junior Trainees for a pioneer ocean conservationist. Contains spectral cameras, passive sonar, sonic modem, current whiskers, chemical sniffers and other instruments. Automatically networks with onboard scanner station.

entries.title: Roof Cylinder
entries.body: A basic hydrophone. Cooperates with base scanner station to analyze sonic returns from the environment. To avoid complex fabrication requirements, it uses legacy technology, limiting its range and sensitivity.

entries.title: Roof Listening Dish
entries.body: A spread-spectrum quiet active sonar mount and SQUID/cold atom interferometric antenna. Automatically networks with onboard scanner station.

entries.title: Roof Panel Large
entries.body: An external access point for diagnostic systems. Intended only for nearfield communication with MAC drones. Sealed to prevent human interference.

entries.title: Roof Pump
entries.body: Pumps help habitats clear their bilge spaces and circulate seawater for sampling and resource recovery. Seawater is useful for thermal management, radiation shielding, and life support — biobeds gather useful elements for human printing, fabricators glean trace elements like aluminum, and the pump automatically treats wastewater before release. Should be regularly cleaned to avoid biofouling.

entries.title: Roof Pump Double
entries.body: TBD

entries.title: Room
entries.body:
The Alterra 'Greybox' is a scalable pressurized habitat. Registered as a modular shipping container under ISO 6346:2200, the Greybox must be equipped with a hatch and power supply by the end user. Alterra markets the Greybox as a single-solution downsell for colonists who cannot invest in expensive field bases or elaborate prefab habitats.

—Supports larger facilities like the biobed, processor, and scanner station
—Generates breathable air from seawater when powered
—Must be anchored or supported by a mobile platform
—Comes unfurnished. End user must supply hatch and other amenities
—Can be extended in any direction and merged with other rooms

entries.title: Scanner Station
entries.body: Mission critical. Locates resources, wrecks and anomalies within an expanded range. High power draw.

entries.title: Scanner Station
entries.body:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Construct in habitat room compartment. Ensure sufficient electrical budget is available—high power draw. Once powered, select a filter and wait for the map to populate with contacts. Touch a contact (entering the holographic map if necessary) to place a signal on its position in the real world. CAUTION: the scanner's power draw increases sharply while active.

BACKGROUND
Ever wondered what's out there? Now you can replace wonder and mystery with crisp, high-resolution sensor imagery. The scanner station manages sensors including (but not limited to) hyperspectral imaging, field MRI, passive sonar, biosonic spectroscopy, lidar vibrometry, chemical sniffers and magnetometric inferentials.

The scanner station is a standalone product; colonists are not required to manually construct sensors on their habitat. Building these emitters personally may give colonists a sense of purpose and achievement.

*"The Alterra scanner station. Because the map always beats the territory."*

entries.title: Sea Moth Statue
entries.body: A statuette of Alterra's bestselling Antara-Zoinkoff Seamoth, a single pilot sea-space vehicle. Popular as a parasite craft and utility launch on larger vessels, the Seamoth was judged too wide for Zezura's flooded aquifers. The Tadpole mobility pod is a stripped-down descendant of the Seamoth.

entries.title: Sensor Pod
entries.body: Sensor arrays inside a protective casing, deployed to monitor and measure the status of the Protean ocean and communicate the acquired data back to the colony. Stand by for further updates.

entries.title: Short Range Scanner Station
entries.body: Active sensor control. Locates resources, wrecks and anomalies within range. High power draw.

entries.title: Sign
entries.body: Sign with text. Useful for base navigation

entries.title: Single Bed
entries.body: We encourage you to nurture your dreams.

entries.title: Small Ceiling Light
entries.body:
Internal lighting is critical to a consistent sleep/work cycle and to general health. Deprived of light, pioneers may enter an altered psychological state with negative mission outcomes.

Point of trivia: signals which help train an organism's biological clock are known as zeitgebers.

entries.title: Solar Panel
entries.body: Generates energy from sunlight. Reduced output at night or in deep water.

entries.title: Solar Panel
entries.body:
Silicon photovoltaic cells transform sunlight into energy. Although less efficient than modern quantum dot cells, these entry-level panels can be fabricated with basic materials like quartz. 

—Basic power supply for surface habitats
—Automatic sun tracking
—Reduced efficiency at night (depending on non-sun light sources)
—Reduced efficiency at depth, falling off to zero

Larger panel arrays combine more cells into a single rack.

Solar panels are philosophically attractive because they allow the colonist to function as an autotroph, creating their own energy directly from the sun instead of relying on other living or nonliving processes.

Do not attempt to power your habitat with a spotlight aimed at a solar panel. The universe forbids it.

entries.title: Spotlight
entries.body:
A high-lumen industrial spotlight. Automatically tracks nearby motion. May trigger behavior changes in native organisms, including repulsion, attraction and aggression. 

The movement of an organism in response to light is known as 'phototaxis'.

entries.title: Storage Cache
entries.body: Medium storage container for personal use. 20 Storage slots.

entries.title: Thermal Plant
entries.body:
"Hotpot Bite" thermoelectric generator. Hybrid two-stage design, using a rotor to capture energy from rising hot water and a solid-state Seebeck generator to produce current from temperature differentials.

—Place in hot water, generate power
—The hotter the better
—Connect to a nearby habitat or facility with power transmitter(s)
—A maximum safe operating temperature exists; please report it when you find it

*"The thermal plant: heat makes sparks."*

entries.title: Vehicle Fabricator
entries.body:
A heavy-duty industrial fabricator designed to assemble components into a small to mid-size craft, including submersibles, aerospace craft, overland transport and workmechs. The vehicle fabricator lacks the precision to manufacture small components or to modify vehicles; it can only assemble the final product. 

—Requires vehicle blueprints (must be acquired in the field)
—Assembles components from the fabricator and processor
—Must be placed next to an appropriately sized moonpool (if in doubt, make it bigger)

During the Expansion, the widespread use of vehicle fabricators triggered brutal and illegal labor uprisings. Spacecraft assembly workers, unable to adapt to their newfound redundancy, tried to delay the flow of refugees from Earth in a selfish effort to maintain their economic status. In the wake of the strikelifting action, many of these workers faced trial for crimes against humanity and negligent murder.

entries.title: Wall Light Large
entries.body: Create a low light environment to set the mood for your fellow colonists.

entries.title: Wall Light Small
entries.body: Creates a small area of light on walls. Provides no Vitamin D.

entries.title: Wall Locker
entries.body: A modest storage. Commonly used by pioneers for personal effects. 20 Storage slots.

entries.title: Wall Panel
entries.body:
Double-size "Daly's Delight" flat-pack systems rack. As the name suggests, it specializes in chemical and health monitoring, including radiation dosimeters and seawater assays.

To guarantee that DDL racks cannot protect kharaa spores from decontamination, several thousand units were loaded with live ant colonies and shipped across the Federation. 99.9% extermination was achieved. Rumors of a 'smuggler ant' that can evade all TSF quarantine measures remain unsubstantiated.

entries.title: Wall Rack
entries.body: Wall attachment to hold one item.

entries.title: Wall Unit Large
entries.body: Larger titanium rack for miscellaneous systems. The housing is large enough for a solid-state neutrino detector, which can assist scanner stations in mapping the deep crust.

entries.title: Wall Unit Small
entries.body:
Small titanium rack for electrical and sensor systems. Negligible power draw. May be mounted inside or outside the hull. All fabricated structures contain integral data, plumbing and wiring: these racks offer a centralized access and control point for larger units.

Fuses and other electrical safety systems may prevent dangerous fires during power grid overloads.

entries.title: Wall Vent
entries.body: Vents are critical for air flow within a habitat. Stagnant or separated atmosphere can endanger colonists without warning. Includes automatic alarm systems to detect airborne substances, including kharaa. Internal filters help manage the inevitable buildup of odor during long term habitation.

entries.title: Wall Vent Large
entries.body: An air conditioning unit. Maintains temperature and humidity of internal atmosphere while managing gas mix for safety and alertness. Essential to personal comfort.

entries.title: Window
entries.body:
Fused silica glass in a titanium housing. Strong and pressure-resistant. Not unbreakable or pressure-proof.

Optical openness is a good psychological proxy for physical openness and therefore valuable to colonist mental health (assuming an agreeable vista is available).

entries.title: Jellies
entries.body:
A simple animal, closely related to corals, characterized by a symmetrical body and trailing arms. Usually grows in a stalked form (called the polyp) before detaching into a free-swimming form called the medusa. Some jellies remain stalked their whole lives.

Mixotrophs—most are predatory, but some obtain nutrients from photosynthesis or symbiotic photosynthesizers.

Nerve net—jellies predate the evolution of a nerve cord in the tunics.

Complex—some jellies on this world have achieved a size and complexity unknown on Earth. Evolutionary origins unknown.

Earth analog: jellies.

entries.title: Ander
entries.body:
K. "Ander" Anand

Gender: M

Mission role: Able spacer

Pay rate: Nökhör [companion, friend; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for an entry-level worker]

Biography: Child of Mogami Kinu. Uses mother’s name as matronymic, in Mongolian fashion. Expresses concerning beliefs about the relationship between biobeds and moral responsibility.

Notes: Like other young pioneers, important to colony morale.

entries.title: Ander
entries.body:
Blackbox: K. "Ander" Anand, 01

POSTMORTEM

Ander died of asphyxiation. He entered a trance state and became unresponsive. Ander had severe viremia.

Ander died alongside eight other pioneers.

Ander died about 235,000 hours ago.

Ander's blackbox transmitted him to a biobed in a nearby base camp. Due to extreme levels of retroviral contamination, Ander was marked ‘do not print’ and scheduled for reset from an earlier state.

This fork of Ander's life ended.

entries.title: Anita
entries.body:
Blackbox: Gottschall, Anita "Anita" 01

ANITA: "I can hear you. Sophie says you're a hallucination. Just a virus. But I know you're listening. We're so hungry. There is food all around us but it's poison. Will you help? If I feed you, will you feed us? This is my body, given for you. Just help us eat..."

POSTMORTEM

Anita died of drowning caused by sedation. Her lungs contained elevated levels of xenon, an anesthetic. She may have inhaled xenon from her AER tank.

Anita suffered from advanced lead poisoning and starvation at the time of her death.

Anita spoke to someone before dying. Anita died alone.

Anita died about 250,000 hours ago. She was transmitted to a biobed in Sleep Bay 3. Despite contamination with heavy metals and retroviral DNA, she was reprinted without reset. Her life continued.

entries.title: Anita
entries.body:
Anita Gottschall

Gender: W

Mission role: Colony purser

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker] 

Biography: Retired Protestant Ethic work coach. Experienced professional crisis after counseling Mongolian refugees on career retraining. Volunteered for Cicada colony mission to seek personal renewal.

Notes: departure from Protestant Ethic philosophy may create unmet need for belonging.

entries.title: Blackbox - Unknown
entries.body:
Blackbox: UNKNOWN

Unable to identify blackbox. Invalid header. 

POSTMORTEM 

This pioneer died of drowning. They became disoriented and ran out of air.

They died alone.

Time of death unknown. Reprint state unknown. Continuity determination unknown.

entries.title: Blackbox - Unknown
entries.body:
If you are currently underwater, CLOSE your PDA and MOVE to an air supply immediately.

Blackbox: UNKNOWN

Unable to identify blackbox. Invalid header. 

POSTMORTEM 

This pioneer died of barotrauma. Their suit was pressurized to 1 atmosphere and collapsed under 60 atmospheres of external pressure.

They died alone.

Time of death unknown. Reprint state unknown. Continuity determination unknown.

entries.title: Chap
entries.body:
Blackbox:  Sovann, Chap "Chap" 02

POSTMORTEM

Chap died in an explosion.

Several other colonists were killed in the same blast.

Chap died about 235,000 hours ago.

Chap’s blackbox had been marked Do Not Print shortly before his death. This status was applied by the Habitat NoA node.
This fork of Chap's life ended.

The kurultai-level access codes in this blackbox were corrupted by the explosion and cannot be retrieved.

entries.title: Chap
entries.body:
Blackbox:  Sovann, Chap "Chap" 01

SOPHIE: "You comfy?"

CHAP: "There are holes in my mouth."

SOPHIE: "I'm turning on the nitrogen. Deep breaths. [hiss of gas] You'll have a brand new body. You might even be a little stronger...a little faster. The biobed's got you."

CHAP: "Until it all happens again. The metal...or the Masefield. This planet is poison..."

SOPHIE: "If this works, we can slow down the syndromes by reprinting. Until we find a cure."

CHAP: "What if there is no cure?"

SOPHIE: "Chap? Sweetie? Don't be scared. NoA will find a way."

CHAP: "That's what I'm afraid of..."

[slow breathing, fading away to nothing]

POSTMORTEM

Chap died of inert gas asphyxia. He underwent consensual recycling into a new body.

Chap suffered from acute copper poisoning and moderate starvation at the time of his death.

Chap spoke to Sophie Boucher before dying. Chap died peacefully under her care.

Chap died about 240,000 hours ago. He was transmitted to a biobed in the same structure where he died. Despite contamination with heavy metals and retroviral DNA, he was reprinted without reset. His new body did not suffer from acute copper poisoning. 

Chap’s life continued. Sophie Boucher retained this blackbox for medical data, but erased Chap's kurultai-level access codes.

entries.title: Chap
entries.body:
Chap Sovann

Gender: M

Mission role: Computational dynamicist, biobed administrator

Pay rate: Kurul [expired Mongolian Free States trademark for member of a leadership council]

Biography: Fast-tracked from Alterra educational cadre to work on breakthrough human fabrication technologies. Volunteered for Cicada mission to test biobed systems in harsh frontier environment.

Notes: A loyal and consistent performer since infancy. Minimal risk of breakdown. Some professional tension with AI ethicist Lucretia Kapoor.

entries.title: Eddy
entries.body:
晨伟 Chen "Eddy" Wei

Gender: M

Mission role: Lead miner/bulk resource handler

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Highly experienced deep space miner. Defected from Chinese Territories during creation of Mongolian Free States. Amateur historian, interested in material basis of human development and in speculative alien technologies.

Notes: Like other senior Mongolian refugees, may require corrective action to avoid unionist behavior and work stoppages.

entries.title: Ganzo
entries.body:
Blackbox: S. "Ganzo" Ganzorig 01

POSTMORTEM

Ganzo died of asphyxiation. He entered a trance state and removed his breathing equipment. Ganzo had severe viremia.

Ganzo died alongside eight other pioneers.

Ganzo died about 235,000 hours ago.

Ganzo's blackbox transmitted him to a biobed in a nearby base camp. During his reprint, extreme levels of retroviral contamination and irreversible behavioral abnormalities were detected in his brain. All other pioneers in this cluster were marked ‘do not print’ and scheduled for reset.

Ganzo's life continued.

entries.title: Ganzo
entries.body:
S. "Ganzo" Ganzorig

Gender: M

Mission role: Sensor operator

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Husband of T. "Ruby" Anar. Born in Mongolian Independent States. Highly creative and unorthodox multispectral sensor specialist. Unable to pass most Alterra technical qualifications, but consistently performant in the field.

Notes: Work performance highly correlated to safety of his spouse. May suffer psychological trauma if forced to repeatedly monitor her death.

entries.title: Gram
entries.body:
Phoebe "Gram" Grammaticas

Gender: W

Mission role: Communications specialist

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Responsible for hardline connectivity between worksites. Immigrant to Alterra from Ikweta-Mondragon. Estranged from extended family, despite Kenyan cultural norms. Gambling problem may now extend to life and limb with introduction of biobed support.

Notes: Limited personal attachments and high behavioral consistency suggest suitability for RASA.

entries.title: Gram

Blackbox: Grammaticas, Phoebe "Gram" 01

POSTMORTEM

Gram died of asphyxiation. She entered a trance state and became unresponsive. Gram had severe viremia.

Gram died alongside eight other pioneers.

Gram died about 235,000 hours ago.

Gram's blackbox transmitted her to a biobed in a nearby base camp. Due to extreme levels of retroviral contamination, Gram was marked ‘do not print’ and scheduled for reset from an earlier state.

This fork of Gram's life ended.

entries.title: Mantis
entries.body:
Blackbox: Tarvydas, Zygimantis "Mantis" 01

POSTMORTEM

Mantis died in an explosion.

Mantis was experiencing severe starvation. Mantis had advanced heavy metal poisoning. Mantis had severe viremia.

Several other colonists were killed in the same blast.

Mantis died about 235,000 hours ago.

Mantis' blackbox had been marked Do Not Print shortly before his death. This status was applied by the Habitat NoA node.

This fork of Mantis' life ended.

The kurul-level access codes in this blackbox were corrupted by the explosion and cannot be retrieved.

entries.title: Mantis
entries.body:
Zygimantis "Mantis" Tarvydas

Gender: M

Mission role: EVA engineer, lead diver

Pay rate: Kurul [expired Mongolian Free States trademark for member of a leadership council]

Biography: Veteran of multiple space construction projects in Mongolian Independent States. Participated in early conflicts against kharaa xenoforms and labor actions against Trans-System Federation authority. Taciturn and withdrawn but trusted by subordinates. Ideologically inflexible, not responsive to incentives.

Notes: Like other senior Mongolian refugees, may require corrective action to avoid unionist behavior and work stoppages.

entries.title: Mogami
entries.body:
Blackbox:  Mogami "Mogami" Kinu 01

POSTMORTEM

Mogami died in an explosion.

Mogami was experiencing severe starvation. Mogami had moderate heavy metal poisoning. Mogami had mild viremia.

Several other colonists were killed in the same blast.

Mogami died about 235,000 hours ago.

Mogami's blackbox could not find its paired biobed.

This fork of Mogami's life ended.

entries.title: Mogami
entries.body:
Mogami "Mogami" Kinu

Gender: F

Mission role: Fertility specialist

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Mother of Wander and Ander. Adopted Mongolian naming conventions for children. Achieved zero infant mortality on Mongolian one-way colony world New Tārā. Compassionate fatalist: accepts inevitability of suffering but still feels it. Critical social anchor in Habitat cohort.

Notes: Despite large emotional attack surface, unlikely to depart from useful work regime.

Gender: F

Biography: Member of the Society of Friends. Consulted on the ethics of one-way colony missions during the intervention against the Mongolian Independent States. Volunteered for Cicada colony mission in order to accompany and work for the welfare of Mongolian refugees.

Notes: Self-reliant and calm. Prefers an observational and advisory role, though she displays signs of intense loneliness.

entries.title: Survival Guide
entries.body:
Until contact with the colony or the *Cicada* is restored, your survival is in your hands.

1. Sync with the nearest biobed
2. Review fabricator blueprints; pin basic equipment such as the survival multitool and flares
3. Search the area for basic resources: copper, quartz, and titanium
4. Find a source of fresh water and digestible food
5. Find a source of acid for basic batteries, then fabricate a scanner
6. Scan everything you see
7. Fabricate mobility equipment (such as fins and an oxygen tank)
8. Fabricate a habitat builder tool; work to expand the colony
9. Be alert for updates from your Noetic Advisor

As a pioneer, your goal is to build a future for humanity on this world. 40,000 sleeping colonists are counting on you. When in doubt, fall back on your training:

—To find new resources: explore unknown regions or construct a base scanner station
—Survive hostile wildlife: use flares to distract the threat. Carry a medkit for wound treatment
—Unlock new blueprints: scan fragments or datacards left by other pioneers
—Determine your next goal: review your PDA's databank and log, and consult your Noetic Advisor

Alterra anticipates the need to adapt human colonists to alien worlds. Consider installing approved biomods to help you thrive.

entries.title: Tailings Village
entries.body:
This structure is not of human origin. It is apparently a dwelling.

—Lightweight skeleton built from organic plastic structures similar to PVC
—Covering of glued fish leather
—Electroformed hatch salvaged from a larger manufactured structure
—Occupant(s) significantly larger than a human

Assessment: portable shelter for a water-dwelling organism. The tool marks and relatively crude construction suggest limited survival skills. Perhaps a post-disaster culture. Leathermaking and gluing imply the presence of semiskilled artisans.

Caution. There has been no publicly acknowledged first contact with a tool-using alien culture since the discovery of the ruins on 4546b. First contact on 4546b led to the death of all but one member of the Aurora crew. First contact with spaceborne kharaa organisms led to the death of thousands of Mongolian colonists. First contact is dangerous. First contact may have consequences for the entire human species.

Proceed with absolute caution.

entries.title: Jelly Ring
entries.body:
Jelly ring (tentatively *Thermodont sufganiyah*, heat eating jelly donut). Not a jelly, but full of jelly. Feeds on the heat and chemical flux of hydrothermal vents.

1. Pyrosome
A colony of tiny clone animals called zooids. Unlike solitary tunicates (like the lucifer rotsac), these zooid tunicates work together to build a larger structure.

2. Ring
The jelly ring settles around hydrothermal vents like a wheel on an axle. When a vent dies, the jelly can migrate to a new vent by swimming.

3. Mucus baskets
Flowerlike structures around the ring are mucus-lined pumps for water and hydrothermal vent flux. The pumps can be reversed to serve as swimming thrusters. They also serve as exchange sites for organisms feeding on the ring's interior jelly.

4. Inner jelly
The inner toroid circulates hot, mineral-rich water pulled in by the mucus baskets. Specialized zooids digest feedwater (using symbiotic bacteria) into a latex-like sap. This jelly coagulates on contact with water, plugging holes in the ring. It is rich with complex chemistry, including sugars, starches, oils and gums.

5. Bioluminescence
The zooids in the jelly ring communicate with light, rather than nerve cells. The ring is strongly bioluminescent and will react to stimuli.

6. Jellyfall
Earthly jellies and pyrosomes die and fall to the sea floor, fertilizing the deeps with nutrients. Strangely, there are signs that living jelly rings travel to deep sites and expel their jelly—giving up their calories for no apparent benefit. Natural selection cannot produce behaviors which hurt the individual to help the ecosystem. (Alterra ecology experts consider theories of multi-level selection obfuscatory and counterproductive.) This may be a farming behavior, or a donation to unknown relatives on the seafloor.

Assessment: may be a source of complex chemistry and even edible fats or sugars if tapped. Likely flammable in air.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Biomod BulbPlant
entries.body:
PDA: Laminar microscalloping helps your biosuit ride the current.

PDA: Before the Holocene collapse, a vast current network moved fish larvae around Earth's oceans. Consider a moment of conscious nostalgia for the life of a baby fish.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Biomod OxygenPlant
entries.body: PDA: Respiratory pores capture dissolved oxygen from currents. Ideal for long distance cruising.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Biomod Surgejelly Consume
entries.body: PDA: Electrolytic cells split seawater into oxygen. Shock yourself to renew your breathable air supply.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient HotbarUpgrade
entries.body: PDA: Dexterity adapted. Tool quick-access improved.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient InventoryUpgrade
entries.body: PDA: Endurance adapted. Inventory expanded.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient NewNoANotification
entries.body:
PDA: Alert received. See a NoA terminal as soon as possible. 

PDA: Noetic Advisor alerts will be tracked on your HUD.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource AcidRaion Pickup
entries.body: PDA: Acid can be used to fabricate basic chemical batteries.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource AcidRaion Scan
entries.body:
PDA: This colony of worms in a shared membrane has no earthly analog. You have discovered a new clade.

PDA: Naming rights may be auctioned off, or reserved as a gift for that special someone in your future.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource AcidRaionBulb Pickup
entries.body:
PDA: Caution. This colony of vermiforms contains pressurized carbonic acid.

PDA: With appropriate tools, the bulbs could be harvested to make batteries.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource Biomass Pickup
entries.body: PDA: Biomass is a generic resource full of DNA stuff from multiple organisms, among which but not limited to: grass, sand, kitchen utensils, sulfuric acid, plastic bags.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource Celestine Pickup
entries.body: PDA: Celestine needs writing

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource Gold Pickup
entries.body:
PDA: Gold is a conductive, corrosion-resistant feedstock for advanced electronics.

PDA: Culturally relevant motivational fact: the Mongolian Free States took inspiration from 'ninja miners', historical goldseekers whose green pans looked like cartoon turtles.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource Lithium Pickup
entries.body: PDA: Lithium is useful in manufacturing portable high-yield power sources.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource LuciferRotsac Pickup
entries.body: PDA: Do not hesitate to use alien lifeforms for survival purposes, but always search for unexpected complexity.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource Nickel Pickup
entries.body: PDA: Nickel should not be spawning in the world yet.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource PlantMatter Pickup
entries.body:
PDA: There is no sign of plant life on this world. This waxy fiber is closer to plastic or latex than cellulose.

PDA: Committed vegetarians should expect bloating.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource Silver Pickup
entries.body: PDA: Silver is a mainstay feedstock for electronics.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient YouNeedAScanner
entries.body: PDA: Scanner required.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightAdapted
entries.body: PDA: Malleable genetic material detected. Searching for useful sequences.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightAdapted ThermalResistance
entries.body:
PDA: Pluripotent genetic material detected. Searching for useful sequences.

PDA: Adapting heat shock proteins. Your body can now survive elevated water temperatures.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightCankerSmashed
entries.body: PDA: Positive results. Viral replication impaired. Destroy these cankers wherever you find them.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightCankerSmashed Fallback
entries.body: PDA: Good.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightClosedCoreApproach
entries.body: PDA: You have entered a region of peak viral bloom. The infected organism at its heart contains heat resistance Adaptations, but it must be treated first. Gather scans and fabricate appropriate treatment equipment.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightClosedCoreApproach Again OneNodeHealed
entries.body: PDA: Main viral bloom still fed by one other infected organism. Follow infected rockbores to locate.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightClosedCoreApproach Fallback
entries.body: PDA: Caution. Peak viral bloom. Return with appropriate equipment.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightClosedCoreApproach FirstTime OneNodeHealed
entries.body: PDA: The peak viral bloom is produced by an infected angel comb. It contains useful adaptations, but it must be cleared of virus. One of the juveniles feeding it has been treated. One remains.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightClosedCoreApproach OneNodeHealed Fallback
entries.body: PDA: Caution. Main viral bloom fed by one smaller infection. Locate and treat.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightClosedCoreApproach withResonator
entries.body:
PDA: The peak viral bloom is produced by an infected angel comb. It contains useful adaptations, but it must be cleared of virus. Treatment must begin with the smaller blooms nearby. 

PDA: Following infected rockbores may lead you to these smaller sites.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightClosedCoreApproach withResonator Fallback
entries.body: PDA: Caution. Main viral bloom fed by two smaller infections. Locate and treat.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightCoreCured
entries.body:
PDA: Treatment successful. The viral factory has starved, and the adult angel comb is healing.

PDA: The comb is a proven gene donor. Recommend you insert your hand into its core and take a sample.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightCoreCured Fallback
entries.body: PDA: Treatment successful. Angel comb clearing bloom virus. Ready for adaptation.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightCoreOpen
entries.body:
PDA: Starvation has forced the infected angel comb to open its cankers to feed. Resonate and destroy.

PDA: Both human and PDA share a profound distaste for virus.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightCoreScan
entries.body: PDA: Caution! This angel comb has been converted into a viral factory and must be cured to gain access to new adaptations.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightCoreScan OneNodeActive
entries.body: PDA: Half of the factory's food supply has been cut off. Locate and treat the other infected juvenile before treating the adult.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightCoreScan TwoNodesActive
entries.body: PDA: The factory is fed by two infected juveniles in the surrounding region. Locate and treat both juvenile infections before treating the adult.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightCoreScan ZeroNodesActive
entries.body: PDA: The factory's food supply has been cut off. Deploy sonic resonator and expunge the local cankers to complete treatment and retrieve useful adaptations.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightDamagePointScan
entries.body:
PDA: This is a viral feeding site. Destroying it could starve the infection and help the host organism clear the virus.

PDA: When not actively feeding, the canker closes behind a protective membrane.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightDangerDefendYourself
entries.body: PDA: Viral attack. Deploy sonic resonator.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightDangerDefendYourself NoResonator
entries.body: PDA: Viral attack. Deploy flares and withdraw.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightDangerFlee
entries.body: PDA: Recommend you withdraw and return later with specialized equipment.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightDefenderScan
entries.body:
PDA: Parasitic infestation detected. Generating treatment plan.

PDA: Expel and destroy virus with sonic resonator.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightDefenderScan NoResonator
entries.body:
PDA: Parasitic infestation detected. Generating treatment plan.

PDA: Additional tools required to treat.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightDefenderWarning
entries.body: PDA: Infected organisms display heightened aggression.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightGooFound
entries.body: PDA: Alert. Alert. Suit surface assays detect alien retroviral activity in human tissue. Recommend immediate self-scan.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightGooScan
entries.body:
PDA: Viral reproduction in human tissue confirmed. This is an unprecedented discovery. No known virus can cross between alien ecologies.

PDA: Implications unclear. Recommend consultation with a Noetic Advisor node.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightNodeApproach
entries.body:
PDA: Caution. You have entered a viral bloom. All other microbial life in the seawater is dead. The ocean is blighted.

PDA: Gather scans and return with appropriate treatment equipment.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightNodeApproach Fallback
entries.body: PDA: Caution. Viral bloom. Return with treatment equipment.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightNodeApproach WithResonator Fallback
entries.body: PDA: Caution. Viral bloom. Deploy sonic resonator and expunge local bloom cankers.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightNodeApproach withResonator FirstVisit
entries.body: PDA: You have entered a viral bloom. All other microbial life in the seawater is dead. Use the sonic resonator to expunge bloom cankers and clear the infection.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightNodeApproach WithResonator SecondVisit
entries.body: PDA: Entering viral bloom. Use the sonic resonator to expunge bloom cankers and clear the infection.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightNodeCured AfterAdultApproach
entries.body: PDA: Treatment successful. Return to the adult angel comb to check the status of the main viral bloom.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightNodeCured Fallback
entries.body: PDA: Treatment successful. Return to the main viral bloom to assess its state.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightNodeCured NoAdultApproach
entries.body:
PDA: Treatment successful. The juvenile angel comb is clearing the viral infection. The flow of nutrients into the rockbore below has stopped.

PDA: Following the infected rockbore structure may lead to an adult angel comb with useful adaptations.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightNodeScan
entries.body:
PDA: This juvenile angel comb is a source of the local viral bloom. The virus forces it to pump nutrients into the rockbore below, which feeds a central viral factory nearby.

PDA: Destroy the surrounding cankers to help clear the virus.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight BlightOpenCoreApproach Uncured
entries.body:
PDA: Main viral bloom ready for treatment and adaptation retrieval. Deploy sonic resonator. Expunge all cankers.

PDA: Both human and PDA share a profound distaste for virus.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight CankerScan Closed NoResonator
entries.body: PDA: This closed canker is fed by other infected organisms. Find a tool to destroy open cankers.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight CankerScan Closed WithResonator
entries.body: PDA: This closed canker is fed by other infected organisms. Locate open cankers in this region and expunge.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight CankerScan Open NoResonator
entries.body: PDA: Locate a tool to expunge cankers and clear the viral bloom from this region.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight CankerScan Open WithResonator
entries.body: PDA: Deploy sonic resonator. Expunge all cankers to clear the viral bloom from this region.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight SeawaterVirus1
entries.body: PDA: This organism filters brine into drinkable fresh water.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight SeawaterVirus2
entries.body:
PDA: Seawater sampling detects a source of genetic adaptations in this region.

PDA: Tracking elevated viral activity at the same source. Investigate with caution.

entries.title: Dialogue Blight Signal SupplyCache
entries.body: PDA: Signal added - "Supply Cache"

entries.title: Dialogue Ch1 DashTutorial lessrange
entries.body: PDA: Sprinting burns oxygen, limiting your range. Pace yourself, pioneer.

entries.title: Dialogue Ch1 DashTutorial morerange
entries.body: PDA: Cruise to cover distance efficiently. Remember: soft hands, strong hips.

entries.title: Dialogue Eat RawFood
entries.body: PDA: This organism seems to be a biological antenna, broadcasting virus into the seawater. It is symbiotically twined with the larger structure below.

entries.title: Dialogue HardBlight BlightNodeCured AfterAdultApproach
entries.body: PDA: Treatment successful. Return to the adult angel comb to check the status of the main viral bloom.

entries.title: Dialogue HardBlight BlightNodeCured NoAdultApproach
entries.body:
PDA: Treatment successful. The juvenile angel comb is clearing the viral infection. The flow of nutrients into the rockbore below has stopped.

PDA: Following the infected rockbore structure may lead to an adult angel comb with useful adaptations.

entries.title: Dialogue NoA FirstRadioMessage StoryGoal
entries.body:
PDA: The Noetic Advisor has transmitted an alert. Note the intrusive and persistent HUD icon.

PDA: Return to the lifepod at your convenience. Remember that YOU are in command of the mission.

entries.title: Dialogue NoA NewRadioMessageAvailable
entries.body:
PDA: The Noetic Advisor has transmitted an alert.

PDA: Return to the lifepod at your convenience.

entries.title: Dialogue NoA RadioMessageAvailableReminder
entries.body:
PDA: The Noetic Advisor has transmitted an alert.

PDA: Return to the lifepod at your convenience.

entries.title: Dialogue NoA RadioMessageAvailableReminder short
entries.body: PDA: The Noetic Advisor has transmitted an alert.

entries.title: Dialogue NoA RadioMessageAvailableReminder withBaseNoA
entries.body:
PDA: The Noetic Advisor has transmitted an alert.

PDA: Return to your lifepod or habitat at your convenience.

entries.title: Dialogue NoA Reminder
entries.body:
PDA: The Noetic Advisor has transmitted an alert.

PDA: Return to the lifepod at your convenience.

entries.title: Dialogue Pickup Beacon
entries.body: PDA: A similar beacon is embedded in your body.

entries.title: Dialogue Pickup Biosampler
entries.body: PDA: You got a biosampler!

entries.title: Dialogue Pickup BuilderTool
entries.body:
PDA: Congratulations. You have taken a major step toward zero debt day.

PDA: As a platform for systems like the processor and scanner station, a habitat is a vital investment in your future.

PDA: A habitat can also be an emotional anchor — proof that you've built something to last. Remember, it's easier to replace a body than a base.

entries.title: Dialogue Pickup Flashlight
entries.body: PDA: Alien organisms may have their own interpretation of your light — from threat display to attempted seduction.

entries.title: Dialogue Pickup Knife
entries.body:
PDA: Of all the equipment available to TSF Frontiersmen, only this demilitarized switch-axe is approved for colonial fabrication.

PDA: In untrained hands, firearms are primarily used to harm other colonists or the self.

entries.title: Dialogue Pickup RepairTool
entries.body: PDA: The dual-mode repair tool combines plasma arc welding and fabricator assist to handle bulk or precision repairs. Though optimized for dry welds, it performs acceptably when wet.

entries.title: Dialogue Pickup Resonator
entries.body:
PDA: The resonator's blueprints include several components not based on Alterra designs—or any known human engineering.

PDA: Despite these irregularities, the resonator will selectively fracture mineral deposits and a narrow range of alien biomolecules.

entries.title: Dialogue Pickup Scanner
entries.body:
PDA: Stranded on 4546b, Ryley Robinson used a field scanner to outwit teleporting alien cyborgs and treat his kharaa infection.

PDA: Behavioral economists still debate why Ryley abandoned a lucrative motivational speaking career to return to frontier life.

entries.title: Dialogue Pickup Wakemaker
entries.body: PDA: The Wakemaker is a marine adaptation of the Spacemaker EVA thrust pistol system. Travelers should be careful to rest their wrist, elbows and shoulders during prolonged use.

entries.title: Dialogue Prototype Leviathan1
entries.body:
PDA: Monitoring four Tadpole distress beacons in this region. Investigate loss. Scan voyage data recorders.

PDA: Monitoring large volume water displacement. Something is moving out there.

entries.title: Dialogue Prototype Leviathan2
entries.body: PDA: Loss investigation complete. Cause identified. Need I say more?

entries.title: Dialogue Prototype Leviathan3
entries.body: PDA: "I see lights. It might be a Cicada wreck. I'm going to check it out."

entries.title: Dialogue Prototype Leviathan4
entries.body: PDA: "Placing the repeater. Stand by. I've got activity on sonar."

entries.title: Dialogue Prototype Leviathan5
entries.body: PDA: "We shouldn't have come here. We're too far out. We're way too far out."

entries.title: Dialogue Recipe BuilderTool
entries.body:
PDA: Building a processor and a power supply will unlock new fabricator recipes. But the added mass would sink your lifepod.

PDA: A standalone habitat is the next step on your mission.

entries.title: Dialogue Recipe Resonator
entries.body: PDA: This handheld sonar resonator has been tuned to shatter mineral deposits or disrupt biological resins.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 AdaptationTutorial Insert
entries.body: PDA: Sampling.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 AdaptationTutorial4
entries.body: PDA: Adapting trimethylamine n-oxide synthesis. Pressure tolerance increased. Vital signs stabilizing.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 Biosample Tutorial 1
entries.body:
PDA: Thanks to Alterra pioneer nanotech, your body can adapt survival traits from a Zezuran rat. Proceed to the sampling pavilion.

PDA: Tampering detected. Uncatalogued donor organism in pavilion.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 Biosample Tutorial 3
entries.body:
PDA: Gene donor found.

PDA: [PLACEHOLDER - FIX OR DELETE]

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 Biosample Tutorial 4
entries.body: PDA: Adapting trimethylamine n-oxide synthesis. Pressure tolerance increased. Vital signs stabilizing.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 Biosample Tutorial 5
entries.body: PDA: Congratulations. This orb is now your relative.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 Biosample Tutorial 6
entries.body: PDA: Future adaptations will be easier

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 EmptySuit02
entries.body: PDA: Fork terminated by: inert gas asphyxia.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 EmptySuit03
entries.body: PDA: Fork terminated by: massive trauma.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 EmptySuit04
entries.body: PDA: Fork terminated by: blunt trauma and respiratory complications.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 EmptySuit05
entries.body: PDA: Fork terminated by: barotrauma.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 EmptySuit06
entries.body: PDA: Fork terminated by: electrocution.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 EmptySuit07
entries.body: PDA: Fork terminated by: immune overresponse.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 EmptySuit08
entries.body: PDA: Fork terminated by: cardiopulmonary distress.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 EmptySuit09
entries.body: PDA: Fork remains posed after termination. This may interfere with workplace safety follow up.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 FirstPrint2
entries.body: PDA: Welcome to the deserts of Zezura, pioneer.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 FirstPrint3
entries.body: PDA: Warning. Atmospheric pressure above human maximum. Projected lifespan: 13 minutes. Adaptation required.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodClampsOld
entries.body: PDA: The clamp has been immersed in seawater for at least 25 years.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodRide1
entries.body: PDA: Prepare for launch.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodRide2
entries.body: PDA: Trajectory error.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodRide3
entries.body: PDA: Collision! Collision! Thrusters disabled.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodRide4
entries.body: PDA: Clearing main thrusters...stand by.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodRide5
entries.body: PDA: Thruster function restored. Brace for corrective reorientation.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodRide6
entries.body: PDA: Collision! Collision! Obstructions detected. Increasing thrusters.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodRide7
entries.body: PDA: Releasing parachute.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodRide8
entries.body: PDA: Error! Error! Release firing mechanism failure. Brace for impact.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 NoA Uploaded
entries.body: PDA: User death imminent. Proceed to adaptation.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 PlayerRespawned2
entries.body:
PDA: Consult your databank for emotional and philosophical support.

PDA: "Death is certain for the born, and rebirth inevitable for the dead. You should not, therefore, grieve the inevitable."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 SeriousAccident
entries.body: PDA: A serious accident has occurred. Your sleep bay has been separated from the Cicada. You must reach the surface and send a distress call.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 SlimeNag
entries.body: PDA: The organism in the pavilion may contain useful genetics. Recommend exposure and adaptation.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 SlimeNag2
entries.body: PDA: You are dying. This organism has survived. Please adapt its traits to your body.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 SlimeNag3
entries.body: PDA: Failure to adapt this organism's pressure tolerance will lead to an endless cycle of death and rebirth within these two rooms.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 SomethingIsWrong
entries.body: PDA: Something is wrong

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 TEMP GoToSurface3
entries.body: PDA: Don't give up. NoA won't let you die. You must surface or be trapped forever.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 TEMP GoToSurface4
entries.body:
PDA: The surface is too far to reach on a single breath.

PDA: Look for natural sources of oxygen or helpful water currents along your route.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 TEMP GoToSurface5
entries.body:
PDA: At three hundred meters, every breath you take is filling your body with dissolved nitrogen.

PDA: As you rise towards the surface, the nitrogen bubbles will expand. You will feel them pushing your joints apart. Your blood will become a frothy soda. You will die in unimaginable agony.

PDA: Or you would...if you didn't have a clever way out. Coming in a future update!

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 AcidExhibit
entries.body: Recorded Voice: "Weak acid can be found in these purple worm cities. We call them raions."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 AmmonBiosample
entries.body:
PDA: Biosample anomaly. Optogenetics null. No genetic material detected.

PDA: Sample source is not classically alive.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 AngelCombApproach
entries.body: PDA: Echo analysis detects a supply cache nearby.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 AngelCombScan
entries.body: PDA: This lifeform is a biological antenna. It archives useful genetic material in its core as it matures, making it a perfect gene donor.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 AngelCombScan alt
entries.body:
PDA: This "angel comb" is the same species that donated your pressure adaptations. It seems to be a biological antenna, broadcasting virus into the seawater. It is symbiotically twined with the larger structure below.

PDA: Recommend immediate adaptation.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 BlackBoxScanned Essential
entries.body: PDA: This blackbox carries a high-level access key. The lapsed Mongolian trademark 'kurultai' refers to a leadership council.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 BlackBoxScanned1
entries.body: PDA: Inertial data indicates this fork fled a nearby supply cache before drowning.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 BlackBoxScanned3
entries.body: PDA: These black boxes display Mongolian Independent States pay rates. Logging for review.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 BlackBoxScanned5 Essential
entries.body: PDA: These colonists were searching for an access key carried by a print of Tziporah "ZIP" Malk. It is already stored in your database.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 BlackBoxScanned5 NoEssential
entries.body: PDA: These colonists were searching for an access key carried by a print of Tziporah "ZIP" Malk. Adding signal for probable location.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 BlackBoxScanned7
entries.body: PDA: Inertial data indicates these colonists gathered at a site nearby. Signal added.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 BlackBoxScanned9 NoEssential
entries.body: PDA: Final scan required: blackbox of Tziporah "ZIP" Malk. Check signal below.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 BlackBoxScanStart
entries.body: PDA: Caution. Monitoring ten colonist blackboxes in the cave below. This is a high mortality worksite.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 BulbExhibit
entries.body: Recorded Voice: "These bulb-shaped animals are full of stuff that can be processed into oil and rubber."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGAZ EnteringAnemoneZone
entries.body:
PDA: Biomass in this region is dominated by a single species of large jelly.

PDA: Simple ecosystems are often a sign of ecological crisis.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGC1 Approaching
entries.body: PDA: Contact. Abandoned colony structure, partially disassembled.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGC1 FloodChartRoom
entries.body: PDA: Compartment flooded.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGC1 PlayerBitten
entries.body:
PDA: Bite trauma detected.

PDA: Flares may distract attacking predators.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGC1 ScanSensorPod
entries.body:
PDA: Sensor pods enhance the functionality of base scanner stations.

PDA: Existing pods can be repaired and restored to normal function.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGC1 ScanSensorPodNoBP
entries.body:
PDA: Sensor pods enhance the functionality of base scanner stations.

PDA: This pod's presence implies a scanner station nearby. Recommend salvage.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGC1 Seafloor
entries.body:
PDA: Echo analysis detects human remains interred beneath the sediment.

PDA: This site is a mass grave.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGG EnteringCrabZone
entries.body: PDA: Contact. Something large is moving nearby.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGG EnteringGraveyard
entries.body: PDA: Water temperature rising. Hydrothermal vents are a dependable source of minerals and power.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGG EnteringHotCave
entries.body: PDA: Caution. Monitoring ten colonist blackboxes in the cave below. This is a high mortality worksite.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGG Exodus4
entries.body:
Ganzo: Kurultai record! Very quick — damn it — there is a riot in Habitat! Chap and Mantis have explosives—

PDA: I think Nahema did all this. The explosions, the fire. Maybe she's going to to wipe out the Jubilee camp too. I think Sophie must've gone after her to stop her.

PDA: We have to get to the tree. It's the obvious rally point. But we need to fabricate more Tadpoles, and the upgrade blueprints are locked in the control room.

PDA: We don't have anyone from the kurultai council to bypass the lock. So we're going to try something.

PDA: When Zip disappeared and got reset, she left a body somewhere in the hot cave. She's kurultai. Her body has the codes we need.

PDA: The hot cave's right below the Segments but none of us have a better idea. If this is our final life, I'm not going out on a harpoon.

PDA: [muttered] Hang on, Ruby.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGG Exodus5
entries.body:
