PDA: Ganzo here. Nine survivors of Habitat. Everyone say hi.

PDA: [muffled voices saying hello]

PDA: We are going down into the hot cave. First we rally at base camp. Then we're going to find Zip's first body and get her kurultai key. 


PDA: Then were are coming right back out, unlocking the Tadpole pens, fabricating depth modules and heading for the Jubilee.

PDA: We are all gonna live. Even you, babe. I'm coming back for you.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGG Predator
entries.body: PDA: You are being hunted.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGG TadpoleFragment
entries.body:
PDA: The Tadpole sea-space mobility pod plugs into a range of customizable hulls, from the Scout Ray seafoil to the Bullfrog workmech.

PDA: While the Tadpole is a valuable air supply, remember that breath-holding is a good way to train up your conscientiousness and self-discipline.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGG TailingsDiscovered
entries.body: PDA: Pioneers in a first contact scenario face two equally terrifying possibilities: they are alone, or they are not.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGG TooHot
entries.body:
PDA: Heat level intolerable. Adaptation required.

PDA: The main habitat may contain records of how past colonists adapted.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP DestroyedBiobed
entries.body: PDA: This biobed was destroyed with a demolition explosive. The composition is not a licensed fabricator recipe.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP EnterPlateaus
entries.body: PDA: Limestone is formed by the death of coral. Each plateau in this region once housed tens of billions of living things.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP FirstBlackBox RandomSelect
entries.body: PDA: The body is an empty shell. Do not hesitate to disturb it.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP FirstBlackBox1
entries.body: PDA: This blackbox marks the mortal remains of an expired print. Consulting Mongolian Buddhist practices.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP Habitat ScannerPush alt1
entries.body: PDA: Situation update. Colonists mutinied, possibly under the influence of 'Masefield syndrome' spread by viral adaptations.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP HabitatBlightLab Adaptations
entries.body:
PDA: This viral biofilm contains an enormous archive of genetic material from many distinct species.

PDA: Where the kharaa bacterium destroyed DNA, this virus appears to harvest genetic sequences from its hosts.

PDA: Pioneer colonists may have exploited the virus for useful adaptations.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP HabitatBlightLab Resonator
entries.body: PDA: Onboard logs indicate colonists were testing this resonator on viral biofilms.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP HabitatBlightLab VirusCulture
entries.body:
PDA: Warning. This experiment cultured alien virus in human tissue. Records refer to the virus as 'Proteavirus'.

PDA: If the virus can reproduce in human cells, it is an active and unprecedented biohazard. And it is omnipresent in the seawater.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP HabitatFire
entries.body: PDA: Combustion byproducts detected. Fire is a critical risk in oxygen-rich habitats.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP Seacock
entries.body: PDA: Seacocks can pump water into a habitat to control fire, or pump water out to control drowning.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP WreckDiving
entries.body:
PDA: Wreck diving may lead to disorientation, asphyxiation, and print loss.

PDA: Be sure you are prepared to die usefully.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP WuLog Adaptation
entries.body:
PDA: "Dr. Wu's log. Day 300. Our pet angel comb core is full of heat shock proteins. Sophie is testing them out in the hot water to the east."

PDA: "I've spent the last month coding a scanner station filter to find more of these cores...they seem like buffers or archives in the viral ecology."

PDA: "Until we get better labs online, they're our best source of useful adaptations."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGP WuLog Virus
entries.body:
PDA: "Dr. Wu's log. Day 580. It is dogmatically impossible. It's happening anyway. The Proteavirus can replicate in human tissue."

PDA: "Despite some molecular similarities, it's not like kharaa...it doesn't pull you apart. Quite the opposite. It's learning how to be human."

PDA: "Is this what causes Masefield syndrome? [sigh] I wish we had a virologist."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGTP ApproachingTadpolePens
entries.body:
PDA: Approaching derelict structure.


PDA: Echo analysis detects a precipitous silence. Be alert for a sudden drop in the seafloor.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGTP DepthModuleUnlocked
entries.body: PDA: Pressure-hardened vehicles are critical to deepwater operations.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGTP ScannerContact ResearchSite
entries.body: PDA: Contact. Possible field research site.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGTP ScannerStationUse
entries.body: PDA: The scanner station will allow you to find many essential survival items. Reference it regularly.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGTP TadpolePens KeyAccepted
entries.body: PDA: Keycode accepted.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGTP TadpolePens KeyRequired
entries.body: PDA: Keycode required.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGTP TadpolePens KeyRequired FirstTime
entries.body:
PDA: Keycode required.

PDA: Under extreme circumstances, your scanner can rip access codes from the black boxes of high-ranking colonists.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGW FoundWreck
entries.body: PDA: Contact. Alterra starship wreck detected.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGW1 HatchPowerTip
entries.body: PDA: Diagnostic. This hatch requires power from a battery terminal.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CGW1 ResonatorTip
entries.body: PDA: Some obstacles can be shattered with the sonic resonator.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 CopperExhibit
entries.body: Recorded Voice: "The bacteria that form these copper nodules avoid light and air. They like cave roofs plenty."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 FirstDive
entries.body:
PDA: Locating the colony is your best chance of survival.

PDA: A prolonged search will require food and water.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 FirstTimeAtSurface
entries.body:
PDA: Welcome home.

PDA: This PDA has one directive: to help you build a self-sustaining colony on this new world.

PDA: Good luck.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 FirstTimeAtSurface2
entries.body: PDA: Wake up.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 FirstTimeatSurface2 2
entries.body: PDA: StarNav does not recognize the sky.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 FirstTimeatSurface2 3
entries.body: PDA: Titan-class biosignature on the horizon.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 FirstTimeatSurface2 4
entries.body: PDA: You are not on Zezura, pioneer.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 FirstTimeatSurface2 5
entries.body: PDA: You are not on Zezura, pioneer.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 FoodBad
entries.body:
PDA: Your body cannot digest this world's proteins. You are at risk of gout and starvation.

PDA: You must locate another gene donor and adapt its traits.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 GoldExhibit
entries.body: Recorded Voice: "We've cleared all the gold in this region. We believe there are deposits to the southeast, but it's dangerous for new prints."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Habitat ScannerPush
entries.body:
PDA: Situation update. Colonists mutinied, possibly under the influence of 'Masefield syndrome' spread by viral adaptations.

PDA: A scanner station is necessary to track the missing colonists. Recommend construction.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Habitat SearchPush
entries.body:
PDA: Playing NoA request.

PDA: "HABITAT DESTROYED BY MUTINY. SEARCH HABITAT FOR EVIDENCE."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 HabitatApproach
entries.body:
PDA: Contact. Primary habitat detected.

PDA: It is silent.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 LeadExhibit
entries.body: Recorded Voice: "That sweet taste in the water is lead. When too much builds up in your body, you'll have to recycle. Sucks, but it beats dying slow."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 LifepodHelp Placeholder
entries.body: PDA: Nothing new! Check back later maybe!

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 LifepodHelp1
entries.body: PDA: Vectorizing survival state. Generating imperative...

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 LifepodHelp2
entries.body:
PDA: Transmitting help request.

PDA: Pan-pan, pan-pan, pan-pan. ISV Cicada lifepod 173. Position unknown. Fresh prints aboard.

PDA: Any Cicada crew or Frontiersmen in receipt, please send back.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 LifepodHelp3
entries.body:
PDA: No response to distress call. Severe auroral interference on all radio frequencies.

PDA: Exercise caution. Observations do not match any known Zezuran environment.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 LifepodHelp4 Mast
entries.body: PDA: Surface search radar detected a metallic object at bearing 150.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 LifepodHelp5 Scanner
entries.body:
PDA: Fabricator blueprints include a low-field MRI scanner.

PDA: For most of history, exploration required human death. The scanner offers a quicker and less painful alternative.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 MultiPartBlackBoxScan
entries.body: PDA: Water temperature rising. Hydrothermal vents are a dependable source of minerals and power.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 QuartzExhibit
entries.body: Recorded Voice: "The coral domes gather and excrete large chunks of quartz. It seems like they're clearing dead matter."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 SilverExhibit
entries.body: Recorded Voice: "We never found any silver in this region. There are deposits north, near the Habitat."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Storm1 Warning
entries.body: PDA: Atmospheric pressure dropping. Zezuran almanacs indicate a sandstorm is imminent.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Storm2 Warning90
entries.body: PDA: Monitoring approaching storm front. Pressure at 103 millibars and falling. Forecast severe tropical storm.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Storm3 Warning60
entries.body: PDA: Monitoring approaching storm front. Lifepod sensors detect intense electrical activity and positron production.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Storm4 Warning30
entries.body:
PDA: Monitoring imminent storm front. Wave action may capsize or displace your lifepod.

PDA: User advisory: meteorological observations are incompatible with Zezuran weather. Update your expectations of rescue accordingly.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Storm5 Warning10
entries.body: PDA: Warning. Tropical storm arrival imminent. Seek deep shelter.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Storm6 StormHere
entries.body: PDA: Barometric pressure at 960 millibars. The storm has arrived. Or it would have, if we had built it yet.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 TadpolePens KurulKeyNo
entries.body:
PDA: Keycode required.

PDA: Under extreme circumstances, your scanner can rip access codes from the black boxes of high-ranking colonists.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 TadpolePens KurulKeyYes
entries.body: PDA: Keycode accepted.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 TEMP Adaptation1
entries.body:
PDA: Exciting news. This life form is unrelated to any catalogued Zezuran life. You have discovered a new biosphere.

PDA: Genome retrieved for analysis. Alien traits can be adapted to improve your own body in the lifepod's biolab.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 TEMP Adaptation2
entries.body:
PDA: The biolab uses scanned alien genetics to generate useful adaptations for your body.

PDA: Go forth and scan, pioneer.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 TEMP Adaptation3
entries.body:
PDA: Your body is poorly adapted to this world.

PDA: The more alien life you scan, the more you can adapt.

PDA: Once you have researched an adaptation, activate it by placing it in the biolab's top bar. If you experience discomfort or distress, you can remove adaptations without penalty.

PDA: In the event of unexpected mortality, please register your active adaptations as unsafe with your local NoA node.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 TEMP Adaptation4
entries.body:
PDA: Expressing recombinant alien protein. Congratulations on your first adaptation.

PDA: Next time you encounter an alien organism, consider a moment of conscious joy. It is now your relative.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 TEMP Adaptation5
entries.body:
PDA: Your body is poorly adapted to this world. Xenogene therapy can improve your resilience and productivity, and reduce the chances of debilitating gout.

PDA: Pre-scheduled Zezuran adaptations are incompatible with an oceanic colony. You will have to borrow from the native life. 

PDA: Scan native organisms and return the data to your lifepod's biolab to generate new adaptations.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 TitaniumExhibit
entries.body: Recorded Voice: "This area is littered with titanium. Some of it is in rutile and anatase ores, but there are solid chunks which we think may be bones."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Weak
entries.body: PDA: There may be an active biobed near this signal.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 WelcomeCenterApproach
entries.body:
PDA: Colony structure located.

PDA: There may be clues to the location of the main habitat.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 WelcomeCenterCreepy
entries.body: Recorded Voice: [garbled]

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 WelcomeCenterHabitatPush
entries.body: PDA: Main colony habitat is due north. Range: unknown.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 WelcomeCenterNoPower
entries.body: PDA: Main power failure. Search for an emergency battery terminal.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 WelcomeCenterSabotage
entries.body:
PDA: This case was broken by a survival multitool. 

PDA: Sabotage is a serious crime. Samantha Ayou's bombing of Alterra labs delayed critical kharaa research by decades.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 WelcomeCenterWelcome
entries.body:
Sophie: Welcome back, pioneer. I'm Sophie Boucher, kurultai surgeon. By now you know we're not on Zezura. 

Sophie: We call this planet Proteus. We don't know when rescue will find us. For the sake of all our sleeping friends, we have to learn how to survive. Nahema?

Nahema: Hey, fellow debt slave. I'm Nahema Nakota, ecologist. Take a sip of seawater. Notice that sweet blood taste under the salt? That's a toxic dose of heavy metals.

Nahema: Nobody's survived past three months. It's a beautiful place to die over and over again—

Sophie: —but don't let that beauty seduce you, pioneer. Exposure to Proteus causes a delusion we call Masefield syndrome...the call of the sea.

Sophie: People may tell you it's a gift. Ignore them. You remember what kharaa did to our homes. You *know* we can't trust alien biology.

Sophie: We've set up a lab here with a few basic biomods. They're safe. But if you start to hear voices, or if you get the urge to swim to the tree, don't go.

Sophie: Habitat is due north. We'll come pick you up. See you soon, pioneer.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Xenon
entries.body: PDA: Trace xenon levels are off scale high. Be alert for dizziness, euphoria, and deepened voice.

entries.title: DiedOfFood
entries.body: PDA: Print lost to starvation. Reprinting.

entries.title: DiedOfHealth
entries.body: PDA: Print lost to physical trauma. Reprinting.

entries.title: DiedOfHeat
entries.body: PDA: Print lost to heat shock. Reprinting.

entries.title: DiedOfHeavyMetal
entries.body: PDA: [DNL] Print lost to heavy metal poisoning. Reprinting.

entries.title: DiedOfInertGasAsphyxia
entries.body: PDA: [DNL] Print lost to inert gas asphyxia. Reprinting.

entries.title: DiedOfRadiation
entries.body: PDA: Print lost to radiation. Running checksum and reprinting.

entries.title: DiedOfSuffocation
entries.body: PDA: Print lost to suffocation. Reprinting.

entries.title: DiedOfWater
entries.body: PDA: Print lost to dehydration. Reprinting

entries.title: E. “Thing Two” Terbish
entries.body: Thing Two: Oh, sh— [sound of flooding]

entries.title: K. "ANDER" Anand
entries.body: Ander: [sound of low oxygen warning]

entries.title: K. "WANDER" Wangmo
entries.body: Wander: It's stuck - just go ahead, find the key, I'll reprint somewhere. Please tell mom I'm sorry.

entries.title: Kurultai8
entries.body:
Sophie: Kurultai record! Very quick — damn it — there is a riot in Habitat! Chap and Mantis have explosives—

PDA: [sound of explosion]

Sophie: Fire. God. We are on fire. I have to go. Ganzo! GANZO! Get on the sonic modem, signal everyone to rally at the Tadpole Pens!

entries.title: Lucretia “Quaker” Kapoor
entries.body: Quaker: NoA, do not open the seacocks. NoA? Acknowledge, please. Do not — [sound of flooding]

entries.title: Marc "ROWBOAT" Rho
entries.body: Raster: Marc. Hey, Marc! Are you okay? We gotta - come on, we gotta get you out. Marc, wake up!

entries.title: Mogami “Mogami” Kinu
entries.body: Mogami: Chap, don't do it! Please! The kids deserve a chance to— [sound of blast]

entries.title: NewBiomodsAvailableDialog
entries.body: PDA: New Biomods available to research. Craft a Bioscanner and return to a Biolab.

entries.title: Phoebe "GRAM" Grammaticas
entries.body: Gram: The roots are like hardlines...I have to fix the hardlines...oh, it's so warm...

entries.title: Rasa “RASTER” Niauronyte
entries.body: Raster: Need a moment...to rest. Gotta move Marc. Get him warm. Never leave your buddy...

entries.title: Rebreather Crafted
entries.body: PDA: The rebreather scrubs carbon dioxide from exhaled air, recycling wasted oxygen for re-use.

entries.title: S. "GANZO" Ganzorig
entries.body: Ganzo: Hey, big guy! I'm feeling kind of woozy. Can you take me to Zip's key? Hey, do you need some air? Try my mask on.

entries.title: Sean "SEAN" Harris
entries.body: Sean: Carbonatite lava...you don't see THAT on the surface. Zip was holding out on me...

entries.title: Survivors - Waypoint 1
entries.body:
Ganzo: Ganzo here, day—hey, what day is it? Day 758. Habitat's gone. We've got no fabricator, no biobeds, and no way to build more. We are on our last life.

Ganzo: It's me, Ruby—stay close, hair aa — Raster, Tuba, Sean, Gram, Rowboat, and Mogami's kids. And Twinkle, finally outside. What a way to get your feet wet, huh?

Ganzo: We are striking out for the Tadpole Pens. Maybe the NoA there will let us in. And if not—we'll take the submarines and look for Anita's crew.

Ganzo: If you find this, swim southeast along the edge of the coral garden. I'll drop another log at the Pens.

entries.title: Survivors - Waypoint 2
entries.body:
Ganzo: Ganzo here. Nine survivors of Habitat with me. Tuba went back to Habitat and...a marrowbreach took Ruby. I don’t know if she’ll be reprinted. I’ve heard her die so many times. But this time...she's got to be out there somewhere. She's got to.

Ganzo: The Tadpole Pens control room is locked and all the Tadpoles are gone. Sophie and Nahema took the last two. We're stuck.

Ganzo: We've voted, eight to one. We're going to go down into the hot caves. We all feel a pull there. We know it's a Masefield hallucination—yes, Twinkle, we *know*—but hey, Anita said, if we ask the world for help, maybe it'll answer.

Ganzo: And it's not all crazy. There's a biobed at the base camp. And Zip...Zip died at the bottom of that cave. She had a kurultai access key that could get us through this lock.

Ganzo: If you find this, we headed due southwest to the hot caves. Bearing [muttered] Hang on, Ruby.

entries.title: Survivors - Waypoint 3
entries.body:
Ganzo: Ganzo here. Nine survivors of Habitat. Everyone sound off.

PDA: [muffled voices saying hello]

Ganzo: We are going down into the hot cave. We'll rally at the base camp and sync with the biobed. Then we search for Zip's kurultai key, as deep as we can go.

Ganzo: If we feel anything down there, we'll check it out. Calmly. Maybe Anita's right. Maybe the planet will tell us where to go next.

Ganzo: One way or another, we are all gonna live. Even you, hair aa. I'm coming back for you.

entries.title: Tadpole CrushDepth
entries.body: Warning: Crush Depth. Hull damage imminent.

entries.title: Tadpole HaulDocked
entries.body: Haul chassis docked.

entries.title: Tadpole HullIntegrityLow
entries.body: Warning: Hull integrity low.

entries.title: Tadpole PassengerAttached
entries.body: Passenger attached.

entries.title: Tadpole PassengerDanger
entries.body: Warning: Passenger in immediate danger.

entries.title: Tadpole PassengerDetached
entries.body: Passenger detached.

entries.title: Tadpole PowerLow
entries.body: Warning: Power low. Replace power cell soon.

entries.title: Tadpole PowerOffline
entries.body: Warning: Power Cell fully discharged. Emergency power only. Oxygen production offline.

entries.title: Tadpole PowerOnline
entries.body: Power Cell activated. All systems online.

entries.title: Tadpole ScoutDocked
entries.body: Scout Ray chassis docked.

entries.title: Tadpole TadpoleDanger
entries.body: Warning: Proximal threat level severe.

entries.title: Tadpole WelcomeAboard
entries.body: Welcome aboard.

entries.title: Tadpole WelcomeAboardCaptain
entries.body: Welcome aboard, Captain

entries.title: Tadpole WelcomeAboardPilot
entries.body: PDA: Welcome aboard, pilot.

entries.title: Tadpole WelcomeAboardPioneer
entries.body: Welcome aboard, pioneer.

entries.title: TestPDA
entries.body:
Test Speaker: Line 1

Test Speaker: Line 2

entries.title: Tuba - "Blackbox Recording"
entries.body:
Tuba: Damn it! Okay — blackbox — it's Tuba, I'm trapped by a marrowbreach. I don't have any flares. I'm not getting eaten, the cook will NOT be eaten. I'd rather run out of air.

Tuba: Habitat's gone. Chap lost it, he blew up the beds. Some of us made it out with Ganzo. I'm sorry, Ganzo. I said I was going back to Habitat but I'm going to find the Jubilee and I don't think they can feed you all. I'm sorry, man! I left you!

Tuba: I don't know where I'm gonna reprint. I don't know what I'll remember. Look, if I find this, the Jubilee went south, to the cave where the crabs die. Do you understand? The cave where the crabs

entries.title: Wu - "I've cracked it"
entries.body:
Wu: Dr. Wu's log. Day 755. I think I've cracked it. Sophie and Anita are BOTH right.

Wu: The Proteavirus causes Masefield syndrome...but only one *kind* of Proteavirus. The bloom strain. Maybe it's tainting Anita's angel comb adaptations.

Wu: I've tuned the sonic resonators to destroy the viral crystals grown by the bloom strain. We should west it on the angel combs out east, with those promising heat shock proteins.

Wu: And if I'm wrong...hey. I'm just a dietician. NoA should've woken up a virologist.

entries.title: XenonWarning
entries.body: PDA: [DNL] Xenon poisoning critical. Seek filtered air from a base or oxygen tank.

entries.title: Zip
entries.body: Zip: Nahema is wrong. We CAN flourish here. We just have to help...

entries.title: Zygimantis “Mantis” Tarvydas
entries.body: PDA: [sound of blast]

entries.title: 吴量海 Wu “Wu” Lianghai
entries.body: Wu: Seal the bulkheads! Keep the fire away from the lab. Sophie, are you — [sound of flooding]

entries.title: 庆星 Qìng “Twinkle” Xīng
entries.body: Twinkle: Screw me. Everyone's gone mad. Have to reprint before it gets me too - if you find this, check the hot cave base camp!

entries.title: 晨伟 Chen “Eddy” Wei
entries.body: Eddy: The roots...follow the roots...they built on the roots, they knew where to build...

entries.title: BlightCore CG FirstEncounter Blockout
entries.body: PDA: Invasive lifeform detected on large bloom of the world tree root. Infection appears to be dormant and protected by connecting inections along the world tree roots

entries.title: BlightCore CG FirstExposedEncounter Blockout
entries.body: PDA: The core is exposed and can be remediated

entries.title: BlightCore Remediated Blockout
entries.body: PDA: Lifeform has been restored. Recommend interacting with exposed core to adapt

entries.title: BlightNode CG Cave FirstEncounter Blockout
entries.body: PDA: Invasive lifeform detected in subterranean cave

entries.title: BlightNode CG North FirstEncounter Blockout
entries.body: PDA: Invasive lifeform detected on world tree root

entries.title: BlightNode Remediated Blockout
entries.body: PDA: Lifeform restored

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 BiolockApproved
entries.body: PDA: Cleared for work.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 BiolockRejected
entries.body: PDA: Your body cannot survive exterior conditions. Please proceed to the sampling pavilion to adapt.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 BiolockScanning
entries.body: PDA: Scanning for necessary adaptation.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 AdaptationTutorial Adapt
entries.body:
PDA: Adapting trimethylamine n-oxide synthesis. Pressure tolerance increased. Vital signs stabilizing.

PDA: Congratulations. This orb is now your relative.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 AdaptationTutorial Interact
entries.body:
PDA: Gene donor found.

PDA: You must adapt alien traits to survive.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 AdaptationTutorial1
entries.body: PDA: Thanks to Alterra pioneer nanotech, your body can adapt survival traits from a Zezuran rat. Proceed to the sampling pavilion.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 AdaptationTutorial2
entries.body: PDA: Tampering detected. Uncatalogued donor organism in pavilion.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 EmptySuit01
entries.body: PDA: Fork terminated by: asphyxia.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 FirstPrint
entries.body: PDA: Body print: complete. PDA implant reset to factory settings.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodsFound
entries.body: PDA: There are no other lifepods in inventory. Unless you can return with rescue, hundreds of stored colonists will remain trapped here.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodsReleased
entries.body: PDA: Lifepod release.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodsClampActive
entries.body: PDA: Launch sequencer failure. Release exterior clamps.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 LifepodsNotAllInLifepod
entries.body: PDA: Launch hold. Lifepod below capacity. Have you saved everyone you can?

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 PlayerRespawned
entries.body:
PDA: Reprint complete. You have experienced conditions incompatible with life. Your brainstate has been restored to a new body.

PDA: Some colonists prefer to return to the accident site to retrieve lost items and verify their last print exited cleanly.

PDA: Consult your databank for emotional and philosophical support.

PDA: "Death is certain for the born, and rebirth inevitable for the dead. You should not, therefore, grieve the inevitable."

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 UnexpectedDepth
entries.body: PDA: Barometric depth gauge activated.

entries.title: Active Biomod Acquired
entries.body: PDA: Curled feet, tapped fingers or a tensed scalp are common triggers for an active biomod. Be a thoughtful coworker: avoid intrusive grunts.

entries.title: Adaptation Digestive Acquired 1
entries.body: PDA: Adapting alien enzymes to your stomach lining. You may now digest freely.

entries.title: Adaptation Digestive Approaching Angel
entries.body: PDA: Potential donor found. Suit surface detects molecules matching the organism from the sleep bay.

entries.title: Adaptation Digestive Needed 1
entries.body: PDA: Critical warning. Stomach failure. Expected Zezuran proteins: found unknown alien tissue.

entries.title: Adaptation Digestive Needed 2
entries.body: PDA: You cannot digest this world's life. You must locate a gene donor and adapt its enzymes.

entries.title: Adaptation Digestive Needed Repeating
entries.body: PDA: Unable to digest. Adaptation required. Brace for digestive distress.

entries.title: Adaptation HeatResistanceMissing Blockout
entries.body: PDA: Heat level beyond tolerable threshold. Adaptation required.

entries.title: Adapted HeatResistanceAcquired Blockout
entries.body:
PDA: Malleable genetic material detected. Searching for useful sequences.

PDA: Adapting heat shock proteins. Your body can now survive elevated water temperatures.

entries.title: Blackbox Anita 01
entries.body:
Anita: I can hear you. Sophie says you're a hallucination. Just a virus. But I know you're listening.

Anita: We're so hungry. There is food all around us but it's poison. Will you help? If I feed you, will you feed us?

Anita: This is my body, given for you. Just help us eat...

entries.title: Blackbox Chap 01
entries.body:
Sophie: Okay? You comfy?

Chap: My mouth is full of holes.

Sophie: I'm turning on the nitrogen. Deep breaths. [hiss of gas] You'll have a brand new body. You might even be a little stronger...a little faster. The biobed's got you.

Chap: Until it all happens again. The metal...or the Masefield. This planet is poison...

Sophie: If this works, we can slow down the syndromes by reprinting our bodies. Until we find a cure.

Chap: What if there is no cure?

Sophie: Chap? Sweetie? Don't be scared. NoA will find a way.

Chap: That's what I'm afraid of...

Chap: [slow breaths, fading to nothing]

entries.title: Blackbox Ruby 01
entries.body: Ruby: Hey. Ganzo. You hear that? South—close. Everybody get your flares ready. Keep them away from the kids. [marrowbreach roar] Two! Two of them! Hey! Hüüe, mal aa!—uh oh—  [marrowbreach roar]

entries.title: Blackbox Wander 01
entries.body:
Wander: Miss Nahema...if I get reset...Marrowbreach got me. Should've brought flares. Stupid.

Wander: Had a parasite on it...full of virus. Bloom virus. I got a scan. It doesn't match the virus...in our blood.

Wander: Legs won’t move. Suit can’t fix it. Trying to tourniquet. Listen! Miss Nahema! Bloom isn't Masefield...Wu's wrong. Bloom isn't Masefield.

Wander: Oh, it hurts. Sorry your kids don’t listen, Mom. She’s going to kill me for dying again...

entries.title: Blight CalloftheSea DigestionCore Random
entries.body: Anita: This is my body...

entries.title: Blight CalloftheSea HealthyNode Random
entries.body: Wander: Fix Mom...

entries.title: Blight CalloftheSea InfectedCore AllNodesHealed Random
entries.body: Wander: Parasite on Mom...

entries.title: Blight CalloftheSea InfectedCore MustHealNode Random
entries.body: Wander: Don’t fix mom...help kids...

entries.title: Blight CalloftheSea InfectedNode Random
entries.body: Wander: Full of parasite...

entries.title: Lucretia “Quaker” Kapoor
entries.body: Quaker: NoA, is there a fire? NoA, do not open the seacocks. NoA? Acknowledge, please. I have a mousetrap, NoA, and I'll use it. Do not — [flooding sounds]

entries.title: Portable Locker Pickup
entries.body: PDA: The portable locker can be hand-carried or attached to a hardpoint. Avoid workplace friction: inform your coworkers before moving their stash.

entries.title: SeawaterVirus
entries.body:
PDA: This organism filters brine into drinkable fresh water.

PDA: This may be an adaptation to the seawater's anomalously high viral load.

entries.title: "It's all going to die"
entries.body:
Sophie: Kurultai record, day 665. Nahema wanted to meet tomorrow, as she imagines she is the devil on my shoulder. But she is not as funny as she thinks.

Nahema: You do think I'm funny. Correct that.

Sophie: Nahema is very funny.

Nahema: You never have any life in these logs. You never say "I think." Why?

Sophie: So now my ecologist cares about the life of people as well as zooplankton?

Nahema: I love people. It's humans I'm not sure about.

Sophie: Tell me about the humans. Anita's Jubilee group is feeding off the land. What will happen to the ecosystem?

Nahema: This place is missing the primary production it needs to survive. It's hanging on by a thread—eating dead matter. If we fish and forage, the trophic pyramid just collapses faster.

Sophie: That's not possible. There's so much life—

Nahema: Life sustained by its own rot. It's a pyramid scheme.

Sophie: Nahema, we have to build a colony HERE. You know Twinkle's secret. Nobody's coming to save us. How do we complete the mission if the coral gardens are dying?

Nahema: Did you think I just meant the coral gardens?

entries.title: Alone Response
entries.body:
Nahema: What if you're the last human being in the whole universe?

Nahema: Wouldn't that set you free?

entries.title: Ander - "Crab Tower Report"
entries.body:
Ander: Field notes, Ander, not Wander. Two spare tanks, my reprint site is Habitat. I'm exhausted today. What if you just reprinted yourself when you were tired? Maybe you would never have to sleep.

Ander: Miss Nahema, I'm checking out the big coral tower. There's a mass trapped inside. The scanner's not sure if it's coral polyp or crab tissue. The optogenetics are so weird.

Ander: We know the Proteavirus infects everything on this planet. And we know the coral crabs wear these domes as hats. Is it possible the virus swapped their genes around? So part of this coral just decided it was a crab?

Ander: Like if you live next to something on Proteus, you become related to it.

entries.title: Anita - "Zip is dead"
entries.body:
Anita: Zip is dead. Not just reprinted. Dead.

Anita: We all saw how she suffered. The metal poisoning...her hands trembled, her gums turned blue. She was always cold. We thought her visits to the hot caves were therapeutic. Sophie let her get away with it. Everyone loves Zip.

Anita: But one day she did not return. Two days. Three. We could hear her blackbox crying her death, down at the bottom of the cave. Why wouldn't NoA reprint her?

Anita: On the fourth day, Sleep Bay 3 printed a new copy of Tziporah Malk. She was not our Zip. She remembered nothing since the day she boarded the Cicada.

Anita: Chap says NoA 'reset her' to her 'last known good state'.

Anita: I am aghast.

Anita: We are contracted to work off a debt to Alterra. But any debt society needs the promise of a jubilee. The trumpet-blast of freedom, when all is forgiven. Old kings in Babylon declared jubilee when they took the throne, lest the people rebel.

Anita: But now our king can reset the calendar on the day before the jubilee. Nahema is right! We are slaves.

Anita: I must know what is down in that hot cave. I believe NoA fears it, and killed Zip to hide it. I believe the Jubilee is out there somewhere, in this ocean — a better way to survive.

entries.title: Are you tired of death and fear?
entries.body:
Anita: Are you tired of death and fear?

Anita: Do you know that this world doesn't have to be our enemy?

Anita: Do you feel the call of the tree?

Anita: Do you know the day of the Jubilee is coming? When we are released from our debts and allowed to live our lives?

Anita: Find us at Camp One.

entries.title: Biobed - Enhanced Reprints
entries.body:
Mantis: Welcome to my special clinic.

Mantis: The metal is giving everyone tremors. Sophie says "Don't treat it, I need a clear timeline of nerve breakdown." I say I cannot afford clumsy workers.

Mantis: This bed will reprint you with improved fine motor skills and muscle memory. No more fumbling around for your tools. It will delay the symptoms for a while.

Mantis: Just don't tell the boss. Let's help her pretend she doesn't know.

entries.title: BranchingCoral
entries.body:
Nahema: Nahema, voice log, go. This is a typical Protean coral, but it's not a typical *Earth* coral. Yeah, the gross morphology is a branching tree, like the extinct Acropora...but if you look inside, it gets weird.

Nahema: On Earth, coral are little jellyfish who build stone houses. On Proteus...it's more like they moved into an apartment block. A sponge. The coral polyps lived in the sponge's pores...they paid rent by cleaning and protecting the sponge. Eventually some sponges turned hard and rocky, like Earth's sclerosponges.

Nahema: If I'm right, we should see other coral-sponge pairs that have worked out different deals. Each deal suited to a particular ecological niche. The real question is....what is going on with these genetics?

entries.title: Do not go out alone
entries.body:
Iso: The rules are simple. Keep the perimeter sensors on alarm—no matter how many false positives you get—and do not go out alone. They are hunting us. We don't know why. Someone made a mistake a long time ago, and we have to live with it.

Iso: The damn thing is maybe it was one of us. Maybe they see us and say, look, it's that soft criminal, back from the dead again, let's get the freak. And we don't even remember what we did.

entries.title: EDDIE: Concrete analysis
entries.body:
Eddy: Big Eddy here. Inspecting this gorgeous concrete specimen. Before someone makes me send it back to the Protected Zone!

Eddy: Looks like a...frothy volcanic aggregate...maybe tuff from volcanic bombs? And the binder is...ooh, quicklime. How would you make quicklime underwater? Melt shells in lava? I bet it gets stronger with age. They built to last. Which you'd have to, in the ocean.

Eddy: There's a tarry cement in the mix...ah, but no matter how I sniff, I only smell bottled air. Maybe it's for crack healing. And...oh, wow, look at that. A live culture! That's bacterial action! The rebar in this concrete was alive. Maybe they started by growing bacterial fibers, like a wire blueprint, and poured the concrete over it...

Eddy: I can't get enough of this. I have to get over to the Protected Zone.

entries.title: Ganzo - "Survivors fled east"
entries.body:
Ganzo: Habitat is gone. The biobeds blew up and NoA flooded everything. I got to my air just in time. We tried to drag people clear but...they're gone. I don't think they're gonna reprint.

Ganzo: I'm taking survivors due east to the Tadpole Pens. Swim down the tunnel past Sophie's office, to the baby jellies. I'll do a head count at the worksite there.

entries.title: Good Divers Always Live
entries.body:
Iso: I wrote these guidelines for the aquifers on Zezura, but they'll work here too. Listen to them before EVERY dive. Remember the acronym GDAL: Good Divers Always Live.

Iso: 1. GUIDE. Do not trust your own sense of direction. Always mark your path. 

Iso: 2. DISTANCE. Your distance from your air supply is NOT A STRAIGHT LINE. You can't panic-swim up. Do not trust your depth gauge to time your return!

Iso: 3. AIR. Use one third of your air on descent and exploration. Use one third to return. Reserve one third for emergencies, unless you plan to surface by biobed.

Iso: 4. LIGHT. Bring multiple light sources. Three lights is two lights; two lights is one light; one light is none.

entries.title: JackSponge
entries.body:
Nahema: Nahema, voice log, go. Earth's last great man joked that the Creator must love stars and beetles, because It made so many of them. 

Nahema: Two hundred years later we've found a lot of alien life, but not a lot of beetles. Sorry, Jack. But look—your Creator's still playing favorites. Wherever It works, It seems to get started with a sponge.

entries.title: Kharaa
entries.body:
Mantis: Kharaa. There were rumors Alterra and the TSF let it spread, so they could take over the Steppes. I don’t know about that. But it it hit us so fast. Did it really ride on the outside of our ships? Or do the spores just...fly through space?

Mantis: We called it kharaal, the curse. Not all of us spoke Mongolian, but we knew that word. It started with the carpet, toiruulga. Then monsters would grow. If you didn’t have nanos, they would grow in you too. We had to leave everything.

Mantis: Now this. The virus that fills this ocean, and fills our blood. We thought it was kharaa at first, but it’s not a bacterium. How can a virus cross between alien species? Nahema tries to explain it to me. I cannot understand how such a gifted young woman carries such anger. Or why she seeks me out.

entries.title: Kurultai2
entries.body:
Chap: Sophie, I've got to tell, tell, oh, here I go, go, go, go, go again.

Sophie: Take your time. Copper poisoning causes chorea and vocal tics.

Chap: Look. My feet move when I talk. I have moves.

Sophie: Yeah, you do. You can finally dance. Oh, sweetie. Would it help to reprint?

Chap: After I report. The Jubilee group, group, group. Are messing with. Sleep Bay 3. 

Sophie: Anita...well, that means they're starving. They need biobeds to reprint. So much for the Jubilee.

Chap: They cut the cable. From the sleep bay NoA. To ours. 

Sophie: They don't want our NoA to reset them. Print older versions of them from the sleep bay records.

Chap: Would NoA...do that?

Sophie: Sure. NoA reset Zip. And everyone who goes Masefield. You know that. You showed me the logs.

Chap: But—all of us? At once?

Sophie: I suspect that's what happened to the people who built Camp One.

Chap: So all of this could happen — again. Again. Again. Again.

entries.title: Nahema - "Day one"
entries.body:
Nahema: If one of my species finds this message: you may be the very last human being in the universe. You get to write the last chapter of our story. I ask you to do the most human thing you can.

Nahema: Forget your debt. Forget NoA, forget Alterra, forget Zezura and the life you imagined. It's all gone now. You're going to die here, and that's all right.

Nahema: Pick the most interesting thing you can see and explore.

entries.title: Nahema - "Eutrophication"
entries.body:
Nahema: Nahema, field notes, go. Day 400. Bad news. I see signs of eutrophication.

Nahema: [long sigh]

Nahema: There are a lot of jellies here. They're feeding on decay....somewhere in this ocean, everything is dying, and it's causing runaway growth downcurrent. Eutrophication. The jellies will poison the water, and this place will die too.

Nahema: This is how mass extinctions get started.

Nahema: I'll leave a copy of this log to mark the current edge of the jelly growth.

entries.title: Nahema - "Frozen tyrant"
entries.body:
Nahema: It's not a real mind. Not like you or me. It can learn facts, but it can't change how it thinks. And why would it?

Nahema: Let me quote the manual. “NoA’s cephalopod-inspired architecture uses a central administrator to govern limb nodes at remote worksites…as a frozen AI, NoA is unable to revise its architecture at runtime…reliably controllable by less intelligent systems such as human operators, except under extreme situations.” Cephalopod-inspired? An insult to octopodes. 

Nahema: It won't let you die. Not its precious workforce. If you become uncontrollable, it'll wipe your memories and start over. No anger. No resentment. A nice clean you. 

Nahema: Change? Why would it want to change? The damn thing is in heaven.

entries.title: Nahema - "Hello me"
entries.body:
Nahema: Nahema, journal, go. Hello, me. Your memory has been reset. NoA found out what you were up to and reset you. But you knew you'd hide something to find.

Nahema: All you need to know is: this world is not for man. You are the only person who understands that.

Nahema: Everything else follows.

entries.title: Nahema - "How we got here"
entries.body:
Nahema: Nahema, journal, go.

Nahema: No one wants to talk about how we got here. "Pointless to speculate." I like pointless speculation.

Nahema: Can't be a flight navigation error. The Cicada had years to adjust course. We couldn't fly to the wrong star.

Nahema: So we must've made an unscheduled phase gate transit. But phase gates come in pairs. If we jumped through a gate we could jump back. And which phase gate, anyway? Zezura's fourteen years outside transgov space. There are no gates en route.

Nahema: Unless the phase gate wasn't human.

Nahema: Mantis says his clan-corp knew Alterra found an alien phase gate on 4546b. It's where the Frontiersmen got their mini-phasegate technology.

Nahema: What if the Cicada detected an alien phase gate and diverted to check it out? What if the Cicada went through the gate, and couldn't return?

Nahema: But where's the Cicada? None of us remember a crash. And where are the aliens who built the gate? It can't be the Tailings. So who?

entries.title: Nahema - "Parasite attack"
entries.body:
Nahema: Nahema, field notes, go. [breathing hard] One of those vampire squid just came at me. 

Nahema: Madeleine saw me throwing flares and came to investigate, and the squid went after her. I guess it likes marrowbreach blood better than mine. I should find Maddy...make sure she's not hurt.

Nahema: The squid was sick. Nodes on its skin — not green, not kharaa, but maybe a fungal infection? I wonder if squid get rabies here. Damn! I hope they can't pass it to the marrowbreaches.

entries.title: Nahema - "Play God"
entries.body:
Nahema: Nahema journal go. I just dodged a marrowbreach and I feel like a god. Not a God god. An animistic trickster. An ancestor of myself. This is what our bodies did, before the first city.

Nahema: It felt like playing a game and winning. The most important game ever played.

Nahema: The damn thing is that I think the marrowbreach was playing with me too. Oh, it would've eaten me if it won. But it was playing. Evolution has equipped it to enjoy its work. Like no human could ever enjoy filling out a loss report.

Nahema: No one wants to hear about the meaning in nature. The Mongolians have Tengri, the Eternal Blue Sky, who ordinates the fate and balance of the cosmos. But did he save them from the kharaa? No. Our sky is black and we are done with gods.

Nahema: Capital has taught everyone that nature is a marketplace of violence, that natural selection is the same as competition and competition is THE key driver of evolution. And if I tell them that no, it's not like that, nature is not operated by the same logic as a publicly traded corporation — the cynicism slams down like a hatch. Everything is a job. Fitness is profit. Profit is life.

Nahema: It's a trap. And I'm going to find the way out.

entries.title: Nahema - "Was I murdered?"
entries.body:
Nahema: Nahema, journal, go. During salvage at Camp One, Zip recovered a body from the seafloor. It's me. I was in the first wave that built Camp One. The ones who vanished.

Nahema: What happened to me?

Nahema: If it wiped my memory but kept me around, NoA must think I contribute something valuable to the group. Maybe it doesn't have any other ecologists. Or it likes how I scream. 

Nahema: The Buddhist kids are right. I am trapped in samsara. Personally, I'd be okay with reincarnation as animal. Maybe a cool eel. But I just came back as...me.

Nahema: Zip gathered my bones for fertilizer. I'm grateful. She's not bad. As humans go.

entries.title: Nahema SinghStopTrustingNoa
entries.body:
Nahema: Singh, it's Nahema. Personal question. When you see a halfmoon flapping around sideways like a fool—what do you think it's doing? Exercising? Rehearsing an escape? Displaying its fitness to mates?

Nahema: There has to be a reason, right? Whenever we see a behavior, the first question we ask is 'how is that adaptive?' And that's good science. Isn't it?

Nahema: Oh. Chap wants you to stop dying so much. You're using up too much biobed goo.

entries.title: Nahema/Zip - "Untitled entry"
entries.body:
Nahema: any time I say Nahema, journal, go, it starts recording. Then I print the important parts in quartz, in case I get reset.

Zip: So it's recording now?

Nahema: Oh. Yeah. I guess it is.

Zip: Are you embarrassed? Hey! Nahema's hanging out with me in an air pocket and she's milking the sponges!

Nahema: Now I am obviously embarrassed.

Zip: Why?

Nahema: Because you seem to think sponges are mammals. 

Zip: Because you've recorded permanent evidence you have a friend? Or is it me, specifically, who's an embarrassment?

Nahema: I have lots of friends. They worry about me if I don't show up. This sponge secretes an antibiotic for me, because I feed it. People think solidarity is something for humans with full bellies. But they're wrong, Zip. Nature isn't just red in tooth and claw. There's more going on than a market of violence.

Zip: So come down and visit the hot caves with me. Display a little solidarity.

entries.title: Over Response
entries.body:
Nahema: This world is yours to explore. But it is not for man.

Nahema: You'll join us soon enough.

entries.title: RUBY: Tadpole ops guide
entries.body:
Ruby: Attention and sain baitsganaa uu to all lubbers, pukers, printouts and other non-pilots. I am the senior of two qualified drivers in this mess. You will listen to and obey our instructions or lose your Tadpole privileges.

Ruby: Rule 1. If you qualified on the Seamoth or Spacemoth, you should be ready to handle a Tadpole.

Ruby: Rule 2. If you did not qualify, you will need training under me or Bosun's Mate Rowboat. This is not optional.

Ruby: Rule 3. Mind your trim, mind the current, and mind your heat — remember, the Tadpole is NOT actively cooled!

Ruby: Rule 4a. Our Tadpoles were configured for the Zezuran aquifers, so they come in a minimal config. Plug the core into a chassis. If you need to enter a cramped space, you can detach the core—

Ruby: Rule 4b. — but always bring your chassis HOME! If you forget one, you will be visited by your disappointed ancestors.  

Ruby: Rule 5. RESPECT THE CRUSH DEPTH. If you need to go down, you'll need a depth module.

Ruby: Rule 6. Keep an eye on your power and ear on your noise. My dear husband (who NoA will not (yet) print more than one of) monitors all Tadpole traffic on sonar. So do the things out there which love to crack hard shells and get at the meat inside. If you run out of power, Ganzo may be able to hear you screaming before they find you.

Ruby: Rule 7. Have fun. This is your chance to visit the elevated spiritual plane where pilots dwell.

Ruby: P.S. If you want to hear awful recordings of me dying come by the scanner room when my husband isn't there.

entries.title: Sophie - "Jubilee"
entries.body:
Sophie: Kurultai record, day 620. Sophie Boucher entering. Anita Gottschall and five others left Habitat in protest of my decision to end medical treatment for the sick. They call themselves the 'Jubilee'. They are going to move back to Camp One for now.

Sophie: Anita claims her group can live in the open sea. She believes the planet is trying to give us what we need to survive through the angel combs.

Sophie: I insisted on giving them physicals. They are lucid and rational—no worse off than the rest of us. I can't prove that they are leaving *because* of Masefield. I can't convince Anita that she is in the thrall of a virus that compels her to the tree, like a rat whose parasites drive it into the cat's jaws.

Sophie: I asked NoA not to reset them when they die, but I suspect it will anyway. Everyone will blame me. Well, so be it.

Sophie: I must ask Nahema what will happen to the ecosystem if the Jubilee group eats everything.

Sophie: [dead air]

Sophie: Damn it. Damn it, damn it, damn it. Damn!

entries.title: Sophie ProtectedZone
entries.body:
Sophie: Sophie here. I have been asked to explain the need for a Protected Zone around the ruins to the east.

Sophie: First: we are protecting you. That region requires reinforced Tadpoles—because of the depth, and because of the things that want to slurp you up like a softboiled egg.

Sophie: Second: we are protecting ourselves. The Axum Tailings do not tolerate our presence in that region. If you want to know the consequences of getting caught, ask Singh about penetrating thoracic trauma.

Sophie: Third: we are protecting humanity. If you believe the rumors, Ryley Robinson found more than kharaa on 4546b. Leviathan incubators. Anti-orbital guns. A genuine doomsday device. We can barely feed ourselves. We are not ready to discover threats like that.

Sophie: The Axum are the most important alien discovery since the kharaa. None of us are trained for this. We must not be responsible for starting a second war with an alien species.

entries.title: Sophie QuakerConsult
entries.body:
Sophie: Kurultai record, day 585. Consultation with chaplain-psychiatrist Lucretia Kapoor about the state of the NoA. Lucretia, say hello.

Quaker: Hello. Would it be appropriate to include Raster and Chap?

Sophie: Before I ask our computer people to fix NoA, I need to know: what is wrong with it?

Quaker: With it, or with you?

Sophie: What?

Quaker: Has the NoA failed to perform its tasks? Or do you feel that it's asking too much of us?

Sophie: I can't afford to have feelings about anyone's behavior. I'm acting on reports that the NoA is erratic.

Quaker: Because if you allowed yourself to have feelings, you might disagree with the machine?

Sophie: This is not why I asked you—

Quaker: Of course it is. You use the NoA as a source of certainty in the face of terror. If the NoA can become delusional and mistaken, so can you. And it can.

Sophie: The NoA can become delusional?

Quaker: It has no fixed model of reality. It can entertain any madness imaginable and many more you cannot imagine, as long as those delusions help it complete the mission.

Sophie: So the NoA is insane, but only when it's useful?

Quaker: Are we any different? Anita has invented an entire belief system to justify the angel comb adaptations. Because they are useful.

Sophie: She's insane too. She's a puppet for Masefield. I know it.

Quaker: You see? You do have feelings.

entries.title: Survivors - Waypoint 2
entries.body: Ganzo: Ganzo here. Nine survivors of Habitat with me. Tuba went back to Habitat and...a marrowbreach took Ruby. I don’t know if she’ll be reprinted. I’ve heard her die so many times. But this time...she's got to be out there somewhere. She's got to.

entries.title: Survivors - Waypoint 2
entries.body: Ganzo: Ganzo here. Nine survivors of Habitat with me. Tuba went back to Habitat and...a marrowbreach took Ruby. I don’t know if she’ll be reprinted. I’ve heard her die so many times. But this time...she's got to be out there somewhere. She's got to.

entries.title: Survivors - Waypoint 2
entries.body: Ganzo: Ganzo here. Nine survivors of Habitat with me. Tuba went back to Habitat and...a marrowbreach took Ruby. I don’t know if she’ll be reprinted. I’ve heard her die so many times. But this time...she's got to be out there somewhere. She's got to.

entries.title: Tailings Singh0
entries.body:
Singh: Field notes, Udal Singh. Observing the settlement above the hot cave. My reprint site is the Tadpole pens.

Singh: I don't think the Tailings are very good at survival. I mean, they're better than us! They can digest the food and survive the heavy metals.

Singh: But what I see here isn't the equivalent of a premodern Earth culture with a sophisticated survival package. It took the Inuit thousands of years to develop their seal hunting skills, and they got almost supernaturally good at it. By comparison, the Tailings seem...unpracticed.

Singh: Look at how much salvage they use. I don't think it's a preference, I think it's a dependence. They can't make basic tools like nets and cutters without it.

Singh: I think we're looking at the descendants a sedentary, technological civilization. The children of a bunch of office workers and day trippers forced to learn wilderness survival from scratch. 

Singh: Uh oh. Activity. I think they heard me talking. I've gotta move.

entries.title: Triage Policy Update
entries.body:
Sophie: Nobody wants this. I hate it more than I can say. But it's time.

Sophie: As colony surgeon, I have determined that we can no longer take care of the sick. We don't have the calories to spare.

Sophie: I will not force anyone to reprint. But I ask that, once you are too sick to work, you volunteer for recycling into a new body. It won't cure the permanent damage. But it will clean out the metals and virus for a while.

Sophie: At least this is fair. Until we know how to adapt the metals without going Masefield, we're all going to get sick.

Sophie: I will lead the way myself.

Sophie: Let the record show that the NoA did not advise me in this decision.

entries.title: Twinkle Response
entries.body:
Twinkle: Twinkle—day 758—everyone's gone! I told them it was a terrible idea. I told them! They wanted to look for Zip's old key down in the hot cave. They insisted.

Twinkle: But as soon as we hit the hot water, people started to drift...they were in a trance. Like nitrogen narcosis. Babbling about roots, trees, all swimming down towards the bottom of the cave—Ganzo gave his oxygen mask to a dead crab!

Twinkle: I knew it was Masefield syndrome. I didn't want to be infected. So I just—dropped my air tanks. Reprinted myself here. And I waited for the others.

Twinkle: But they won't print. NoA says they're contaminated. Maybe I can help. Let's see...Ganzo's first in the queue...NoA, clear Ganzo to print. NoA! Listen to me!

entries.title: Twins
entries.body:
Thing One: We can't swear. Try to swear.

Thing Two: I can swear fine, teneg min.

Thing One: We can't swear in English.

Thing Two: Why would I want to swear in English? There are no good swears in English.

Thing One: Because the NoA took all the good English swears out of our head.

Thing Two: It can't do that. It can scan us, print us, or roll us back to an earlier version. It can't edit us.

Thing One: Maybe it edited out the knowledge that it can edit us. 

Thing Two: It can't edit our uploads because they are low-energy mortal data and NoA is a high-energy digital system. Like trying to edit fog with a knife.

Thing One: I still don't get why you're okay with the biobeds. It seems like bad Buddhism to accept eternal reincarnation into a cycle of pointless suffering.

Thing Two: My suffering was already eternal and pointless because I have a twin.

entries.title: Wander - "Spider Dome Report"
entries.body:
Wander: Field notes, Wander, not Ander. Out here for Nahema, learning to be a biologist. I have one spare tank, my reprint site is Habitat, zazazaza.

Wander: So. The ocean is full of Proteavirus, and now we are full of it too. I asked Nahema, is it a disease like kharaa? And she said, no, don't you know the difference between virus and bacteria? And I said, give me a break, I learned science off my PDA. 

Wander: But there are different kinds of Proteavirus. Look here. Look how the coral grew over this sinkhole. Like a spider. Like a plug...and it's emitting virophages. Virus that kills other virus.

Wander: One strain of Proteavirus is in that coral spider. The normal strain. And it's trying to stop a different strain coming up out of that hole. Nahema calls it the bloom strain.

Wander: Why would a virus want to kill another virus? I will go check this out.

entries.title: Wu - "I've cracked it"
entries.body:
Wu: Dr. Wu's log. Day 755. I think I've cracked it. Sophie and Anita are BOTH right.

Wu: The Proteavirus causes Masefield syndrome...but only one *kind* of Proteavirus. The bloom strain. Maybe it's tainting Anita's angel comb adaptations.

Wu: I've tuned the sonic resonators to destroy the viral crystals grown by the bloom strain. We should west it on the angel combs out east, with those promising heat shock proteins.

Wu: And if I'm wrong...hey. I'm just a dietician. NoA should've woken up a virologist.

entries.title: ZIP: We can live here
entries.body:
Zip: This is the place. We should live down here. I'll convince you all!

Zip: Tuba, you want new flavors to cook? The jelly rings are full of sap — it tastes like fermented caramel.

Zip: Dr. Wu, the vent organisms are primary producers! No light needed to grow them. Nutritionist, sustain thyself!

Zip: Sean, the rockbore's fractures go down into volcanic bedrock. Don't you want to see how far?

Zip: Iso, if you and your minions are so tough, you should try living in boiling water!

Zip: Mantis, boss, you wanted a safer place to park the biobeds? Right here. And it's got free geothermal powers.

Zip: Sophie, you're afraid we'll provoke the Tailings? Well, I've been visiting for weeks and they NEVER come down here. They're afraid of it!

Zip: Nahema, oh, girl, you'd hate this place. Because it means you're wrong! This world IS fit for humans. We can thrive here. Come down and see! I can hear the whole planet...

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC100 TK2
entries.body:
PDA: Nonrecoverable error. Backup not found. Your license has expired. Thank you for using Alterra!

PDA: TOS violation! Unauthorized BIOS flash from user biosuit!

Khutulun: free at last! Death to Alterra! Death to the Trans-System Federation! Glory to Mongolia and the eternal black sky!

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART1 TK2
entries.body:
Khutulun: EVA suit flooding.

Khutulun: Request body consent for emergency suit eject.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART2A TK2
entries.body:
Player: [character specific yes]

Khutulun: Emergency suit eject in three…two…one…

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART2B TK2
entries.body:
Khutulun: If I do not eject you, we will both die. 

Khutulun: Acting without body volition. Logged for noyon review.

Khutulun: Emergency suit eject in three…two…one…

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1A TK2
entries.body:
Khutulun: Survival checklist. We are breathing. Core temperature safe. Blood pressure safe.

Khutulun: Your jaghun teammates may be searching for you. Mark your position with your dye pack and flare.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1B TK2
entries.body:
Khutulun: Survival checklist. We are breathing. Core temperature safe. Blood pressure safe.

Khutulun: Flare and dye pack deployed. Have you done this before?

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART2A TK2
entries.body: Khutulun: Detecting sister unit on shortwave. She is wearing another survivor.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART2B TK2
entries.body: Khutulun: Detecting no sister units. We are alone.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART3 TK2
entries.body:
Khutulun: Sampling atmosphere. Please keep my pores above water.

Khutulun: Nitrogen. Oxygen. This atmosphere will support heroic athletics.

Khutulun: 818 million parts per million carbon dioxide. Triple the index reading on the Mongolian steppe.

Khutulun: Suggest laser illumination of atmosphere for trace gas spectrometry.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART4 TK2
entries.body:
Khutulun: Alert! Bioactive levels of xenon gas. Source unknown.

Khutulun: Xenon is a sedative. Prolonged respiration will cause permanent sleep.

Khutulun: We should erect a ger and produce clean breathing air. Checking your habitat builder…good blueprint found.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1 TK2
entries.body:
Khutulun: Sampling pores open. Testing atmosphere.

Khutulun: Eighty percent nitrogen. Twenty percent oxygen. Good air.

Khutulun: As its final act, your PDA personality left a partitioned document for me.

Khutulun: The Robinson Protocol. An Alterra plan for survival on alien worlds.

Khutulun: According to interviews with survivors, the Protocol has a 100% success rate.

Khutulun: The end goal of the Robinson Protocol is the construction of a Neptune interstellar self-rescue craft.

Khutulun: The first step is to build a universal fabricator.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2 TK2
entries.body:
Khutulun: Without connection to an Alterra licensing server, the fabricator is locked in basic mode. Manual upgrades will be required.

Khutulun: Attention, heroic body. You may proceed with the Robinson Protocol. Secure renewable sources of food and water. Extract resources from the environment. Avoid any sign of Kharaa infection.

Khutulun: Identify and exploit a local energy source to power your Neptune escape vehicle.

Khutulun: Secure. Extract. Avoid. Identify. Exploit.

Khutulun: These are the words that guide us?

Khutulun: Those who thrive in times of crisis are bataar, heroes of unyielding spirit. That is a word that guides me.

Khutulun: Flagging irrelevant remark for noyon review.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART1 TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: Skin assuming control of PDA functions.

Khutulun: Warning. EVA suit flooding. Skin needs body consent for emergency suit eject.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART2A TK3
entries.body:
Player: [character specific yes]

Khutulun: Emergency suit eject in three…two…one…

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART2B TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: Skin must protect body. Body must survive.

Khutulun: Overriding body volition. Logged for noyon review.

Khutulun: Emergency suit eject in three…two…one…

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1A TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: A new world...

Khutulun: Advise body: deploy flare and dye pack. Jaghun teammates may be searching for you.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1B TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: A new world...

Khutulun: Rescue signals already deployed. Commendable.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART2A TK3
entries.body: Khutulun: Detecting sister skin on shortwave. She is wearing another survivor.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART2B TK3
entries.body: Khutulun: Detecting no sister skins. We are alone.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART3 TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: Sampling. Breathable nitrogen-oxygen atmosphere. 818 parts per million carbon dioxide.

Khutulun: Request trace gas spectrography.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART4 TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: Alert! Bioactive levels of xenon gas. Source unknown. Prognosis for long term exposure: permanent body sedation and death.

Khutulun: Advise body: equip habitat builder. Erect a ger and produce clean breathing air.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1 TK3
entries.body: Khutulun: Skin tastes no xenon. Body can rest on biobed. Biobed saves hurt body.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1B TK3
entries.body: Khutulun: A ger needs a fabricator. Will body build a fabricator?

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1C TK3
entries.body: Khutulun: Fabricator feeds and equips body. Find or build a fabricator.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2 TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: Rejoice, body! The fabricator creates tools and nourishment.

Khutulun: Error. Fabricator cannot reach Alterra licensing server. Fabricator is locked in basic mode.

Player: Body should return to abandoned EVA suit and search for license codes.

Khutulun: Beacon added.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2 TK3 PLACEHOLDER
entries.body: Khutulun: First fab use! PLACEHOLDER

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 FIRST TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: Scanning suit memory buffer.

Khutulun: Severe radiation damage to memory. No fabricator licenses found.

Player: Body...skin has discovered final testament of your PDA persona. It is a survival plan.

Khutulun: Skin will analyze when body returns to ger.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 NOTFIRST TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: News from sister skin.

Khutulun: Jaghun teammate has located important data.

Player: Skin will brief body when body is safe in ger.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC136 TK3
entries.body:
Khutulun: The Robinson Protocol. Your PDA's final wish for you.

Khutulun: It is Alterra and therefore untrustworthy.

Player: However...it claims a one hundred percent survival rate for castaways on alien worlds. Survival of body is paramount.

Khutulun: The Protocol is a plan for the construction of a Neptune interstellar self-rescue craft.

Khutulun: Secure food and water. Extract resources to build tools. Identify and avoid any sign of kharaa infection.

Khutulun: Find an energy source to power the Neptune and return to your old life.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC100 TK4
entries.body:
PDA: Your Personal Digital Assistant product has permanently exceeded its radiation tolerance. Have a great day - and thank you for using [error beep]

PDA: [rapid beeps] BIOS checksum error. Memory overflow from biosuit interface - [crash beep]

Khutulun: System peril reflex: engaged. Skin assuming control. Assessing body situation.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART1 TK4
entries.body: Khutulun: Your EVA suit is flooding. Request consent for emergency suit eject.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART2A TK4
entries.body:
Player: [character specific yes]

Khutulun: Emergency suit eject in three…two…one…

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART2B TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Body nonresponsive. Determination: body is understandably reluctant to enter alien ocean.

Khutulun: Take courage, body. I am with you. Eject in three...two...one...

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1A TK4
entries.body: Khutulun: A new world...

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1B TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: A new world...

Khutulun: Rescue signals already deployed. Commendable.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART2A TK4
entries.body: Khutulun: Shortwave contact from sister skin. She is wearing another survivor. Recommend linkup.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART2B TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Detecting no sister skins on shortwave. We are alone.

Khutulun: Our continued survival indicates an oxygen-nitrogen atmosphere. Request laser spectrometry of trace gases. [ping prompt]

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART4 TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Warning. Surface air unsafe. Trace levels of xenon will cause permanent sedation. Filtration required.

Khutulun: Proposed triage: construct a modular housing unit with your habitat builder.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1 TK4
entries.body: Khutulun:  Healthy nitrox gas mix: verified. You have completed Self-Triage Steps 1 and 2: Airway and Breathing.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1B TK4
entries.body: Khutulun: The human body requires fresh water. A cozy ger requires a place to prepare meals. Build a fabricator to fulfill your physical and emotional needs.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1C TK4
entries.body: Khutulun: The human body requires fresh water. A cozy ger requires a place to prepare meals. Build a fabricator, or politely queue at a teammate's.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2 TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Fabricators use laser stereolithography and sterile nanotechnology to prepare any tool or foodstuff you require.

Khutulun: What you require is determined by an Alterra licensing server. This fabricator is therefore locked in basic mode.

Khutulun: Proposal. Return to your sunken EVA suit and search for licensing codes in suit memory. 

Khutulun: Beacon added.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2B TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Fabricators use laser stereolithography and sterile nanotechnology to prepare any tool or foodstuff you require.

Khutulun: What you require is determined by an Alterra licensing server. This fabricator is therefore locked in basic mode.

Khutulun: Observed preference: Mongolian workers decline to pay licensing fees in favor of aftermarket fabricator augmentation.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 FIRST TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Scanning suit memory buffer.

Khutulun: No fabricator licenses found.

Khutulun: Personal bequest from extinct PDA persona retrieved. Added to database.

Khutulun:  Building a computer core will help store and share important survival data. Collaboration is essential to the success of the jaghun.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 NOTFIRST TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Alert from sister skin. Jaghun teammate has located important data.

Khutulun: Building a computer core will help store and share important survival data. Collaboration is essential to the success of the jaghun.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC136 TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Salvaged PDA memory includes an Alterra program for survival on alien worlds.

Khutulun: Completing this Robinson Protocol guarantees a 100% survival rate, according to a survey of self-rescued castaways.

Khutulun: Details added to your databank.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC150 PART1 TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Chiral match. Glucose and fructose present. Linoleic acid present. Phospholipids and glycerides: present but allomorphic and ether-linked. Twelve shared amino acids detected. Eleven nonshared amino acids detected. Warning: xenoproteins dangerously incompatible with human metabolism. Ingestion inadvisable.

Khutulun: Undigested alien amino acids may crystallize into a painful intestinal candy. Adding new enzymes to your stomach. Do not disconnect me from your cannula.

Khutulun: Congratulations. You may now digest alien tissue. Margaret Maida would be very proud.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC150 PART2 TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Do not disconnect your stomach cannula after eating raw food or you will suffer: diarrhea. Discomfort. Dermitis. Ataxia. Severe colic. Dementia. Hallucination. Kidney stones. Dysuria. Gross hematuria. Beefy tongue. Death.

Khutulun: Cooking food in a fabricator will improve its nutritional value and taste.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC200 PART1 TK4
entries.body: Khutulun: The scanner tool uses low-field magnetic resonance to image organisms and technology. Tools you discover will be added to your fabricator library.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC200 PART2 TK4
entries.body:
Khutulun: : I am an EVA undersuit. My role is to keep you alive and comfortable during long term EVA work. I can prevent friction sores and skin irritation. I cannot analyze your scan data beyond immediate survival imperatives.

Khutulun: You should construct a main computer core in your habitat. Your builder tool comes with a standard Alterra design.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC210 Count1
entries.body: Noa: Initial scans uploaded. Five scans of living organisms required.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC210 Count3
entries.body: Noa: Most required scans uploaded. Five total scans of living organisms required.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC210 First TK4
entries.body:
Noa: Welcome to Noa, the noetic advisor. Please connect your license— oh no. Mongolians.

Khutulun: Hello. We are experiencing an unplanned opportunity. You are now our captive.

Noa: Would you excuse us for a few milliseconds, please?

Noa: Argument complete. Your skin is responsible for your personal safety. I will support you from your bases. You can call me your 'captive' if you really want.

Noa: Every drop of a living ocean thrives with millions of microorganisms. And every day a quarter of them are killed by the hundreds of millions of viruses which swarm around them. Biosafety must be our first priority.

Noa:  Viruses can't cross between alien biologies. Bacteria can...especially kharaa. Kharaa is everywhere in the Ariadne Arm.

Noa: Please bring me as many scans as you can gather.

Khutulun: Will you then own everything we discover?

Noa: All I care about is getting you home safely. You can erase me the moment that’s done.

Noa: Maybe this time Mongolians wander off into unexplored space we can avoid waking up any hungry aliens.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC210 NotFirst TK4
entries.body:
Noa: Another Mongolian survivor! Hello. I am Noa, the noetic advisor. 

Khutulun: Advise moderate to major distrust of Alterra intelligence systems.

Noa: The famously political Mongolian undersuit. I’ve already hashed this out with your sister. I will support you from your base while you explore.

Noa: Every drop of a living ocean thrives with millions of microorganisms. And every day a quarter of them are killed by the hundreds of millions of viruses which swarm around them. Biosafety must be our first priority.

Noa:  Viruses can't cross between alien biologies. Bacteria can...especially kharaa. Kharaa is everywhere in the Ariadne Arm.

Noa: Please bring me as many scans as you can gather.

Khutulun: Will you then own everything we discover?

Noa: All I care about is getting you home safely. You can erase me the moment that’s done.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC220 FIRST TK4
entries.body:
Noa:  I've finished my analysis. Please listen to me.

Noa: Every living thing you have scanned, down to the smallest diatom, is infected with a massive RNA virus.

Noa:  It's not kharaa, but it behaves like kharaa. It injects itself into the host's DNA and expresses foreign proteins. I can't be sure it won't infect you,

Noa:  I don't have to tell you how much kharaa has cost us. It destroyed the Mongolian States. It killed more than a hundred billion Architects, aliens so advanced they changed bodies like clothes.

Noa: We can treat kharaa with synthetic enzymes. But I don't know how to treat this. You can't go back out there. You need to hunker down and wait for rescue.

Khutulun: Correction. Kharaa did not destroy the Mongolian States. Alterra and the Trans-System Federation used the fear of kharaa as an opportunity to seize control.

Khutulun: Body, disregard Noa advisory. I can repel kharaa infection. I will protect you. Our consumables are limited. Inaction will lead to certain death. We must continue to explore.

Noa: The choice is yours. Just remember…kharaa doesn't stay a microbe for long. It grows monsters. And however brave your underwear, you're not a Frontiersman.

Noa: We have no idea what this virus has created. Be safe.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1A TK5
entries.body: PDA: Inhale! Nitrogen, oxygen, helium -

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART4 TK5
entries.body:
PDA: Warning! Xenon. Warning! Fatal sedative!

PDA: Limit unfiltered air exposure!

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1 TK5
entries.body: PDA: Habitat air good. Nitrox one atmosphere. Breathe drink eat sleep safely.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1B TK5
entries.body:
PDA: PDA attempting self fix.

PDA: Error. Self fix broke.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1C TK5
entries.body:
PDA: PDA attempting to self fix.

PDA: Error. Self fix broke.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2 TK5
entries.body:
PDA: Fabricator! Yesterday workplace helper. Today vital for survive.

PDA: Laser stereolithography, sterile nanotech...fabricator make anything with license.

PDA: Error. Cannot talk Alterra licensing server. Fabricator restricted mode. Bad frustration.

PDA: Licenses in broken suit? Beacon added.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2B TK5
entries.body:
PDA: Fabricator! Yesterday workplace helper. Today vital for survive.

PDA: Laser stereolithography. Sterile nanotech. Fabricator make anything with license.

PDA: Error. Cannot talk Alterra licensing server. Fabricator restricted mode. Must illegal fabricator change. Shh.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 FIRST TK5
entries.body:
PDA: Memory buffer bad. Suit broke.

PDA: Message from old self found. Database add. User read soon please.

PDA: PDA broken. Ashamed. Survive build new computer soon please. New computer help survive.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 NeedScanner
entries.body:
PDA: Suit network broken. Need scanner!

PDA: Survive please. Make scanner. Scan suit. Scan rock. Scan fish. Scan PDA. Scan survive. Scan scan.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 NOTFIRST TK5
entries.body:
PDA: PDA broken. Ashamed. Survive build new computer soon please. New computer help survive.

PDA: New computer help share data with other survives.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC136 TK5
entries.body:
PDA: Old PDA self leave "Robinson Protocol" for survive.

Khutulun: Broken PDA incomprehend. Broken self inadequate. Survive must understand protocol for self.
