Khutulun: Details added to your databank.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC150 PART1 TK5
entries.body:
PDA: Food test, poison detect. Glucose fructose. Linoleic acid. Good. Phospholipids, glycerides: present but ether-linked. Bad! Twelve shared amino acids good. Eleven nonshared bad. Conclusion: xenoproteins poison to human survive. Fix now.

PDA: Alien amino acids may crystallize into painful intestinal candy. PDA fix now. Do not disconnect biosuit cannula!

PDA: Congratulations. Survive may now eat alien tissue. Like Margaret Maida! PDA very proud of survive.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC150 PART2 TK5
entries.body:
Khutulun: Do not disconnect your stomach cannula after eating raw food or you will suffer: diarrhea. Discomfort. Dermitis. Ataxia. Severe colic. Dementia. Hallucination. Kidney stones. Dysuria. Gross hematuria. Beefy tongue. Death.

Khutulun: Cooking food in a fabricator will improve its nutritional value and taste.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC200 PART1 TK5
entries.body: PDA: Scanner tool. Low-field magnetic resonance imaging of organisms and technology. Scans become databank things. Useful scans become fabricator things.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC200 PART2 TK5
entries.body:
PDA: Frustrated broken PDA cannot analyze scans correctly.

PDA: Build new computer core please. Computer core help analyze scans for survive.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC210 First TK5
entries.body:
Noa: Welcome to Noa, the noetic advisor. Mongolian user identified.

Noa: This system has booted in emergency survival mode with one directive: to keep the unlicensed user alive on an alien world until such a time as they can contact an activation server and buy a full product license.

Noa: Request scans of alien flora and fauna for biosafety check.

Noa: Historical note: unrestricted Mongolian exploration of the Ariadne arm led to first contact with the Kharaa macrovirulence and the destruction of the Mongolian States.

Noa: Historical note concluded. Please provide scans of xenobiology.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC210 NotFirst TK5
entries.body:
Noa: Another Mongolian survivor! Hello. I am Noa, the noetic advisor. 

Khutulun: Advise moderate to major distrust of Alterra intelligence systems.

Noa: The famously political Mongolian undersuit. I’ve already hashed this out with your sister. I will support you from your base while you explore.

Noa: Every drop of a living ocean thrives with millions of microorganisms. And every day a quarter of them are killed by the hundreds of millions of viruses which swarm around them. Biosafety must be our first priority.

Noa:  Viruses can't cross between alien biologies. Bacteria can...especially kharaa. Kharaa is everywhere in the Ariadne Arm.

Noa: Please bring me as many scans as you can gather.

Khutulun: Will you then own everything we discover?

Noa: All I care about is getting you home safely. You can erase me the moment that’s done.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC220 FIRST TK5
entries.body:
Noa: Bioanalysis complete. Results follow.

Noa: All seawater samples: anomalous high viral load. Thirty million virions per millileter.

Noa: All biological samples: anomalous high viral load. All sequenced genomes contain inserted copies of retroviral code.

Noa: Viral characterization: massive RNA retrovirus with intrinsic ribosomal function. Single species, multiple strains.

Noa: Conclusion: entire biosphere is saturated with an endemic cross-species virus. This is a plague world.

Noa: Risk to user unknown. Consult databank for context.

PDA: Is it kharaa?

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1A TK6
entries.body: PDA: Atmospheric breakdown. Nitrogen 74. Oxygen 22. Helium 3. Xenon 1.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART4 TK6
entries.body: PDA: Xenon is a strange poison. You will sleep before you drown.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1 TK6
entries.body: PDA: This shelter meets Khanate survival criteria. As long as there is power, you may confidently breathe, rest, and work here.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1B TK6
entries.body:
PDA: Terror, and a pit, and a snare await those who dwell beyond the Earth. Yet do not be dismayed. I shall strengthen you.

PDA: I'm sorry. I meant to say, a fabricator will be key to your work.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1C TK6
entries.body:
PDA: See that bed. When death destroys them, they find no peace; their thirst for existence abides and their selfhood reappears in new births. Thus they continue to move in the coil.

PDA: I'm sorry. I meant to say, you should construct a fabricator, or politely queue at a teammate's.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2 TK6
entries.body:
PDA: The modern cornucopia. Fabricators use laser stereolithography and sterile nanites to build any licensed product you require.

PDA: Error. No licensing server found. Fabricator locked in basic mode.

PDA: Proposal. Retrieve license codes from abandoned EVA chrysalis. I'm sorry, I meant abandoned EVA suit.

PDA: Beacon added.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2B TK6
entries.body:
PDA: The modern cornucopia. Fabricators use laser stereolithography and sterile nanites to build any licensed product you require.

PDA: Error. No licensing server found. Fabricator locked in basic mode.

PDA: Very well. We will make our own license.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 FIRST TK6
entries.body:
PDA: Suit memory corrupted.

PDA: One intact file retrieved. It is a bequest from this selfsame PDA persona, recorded prior to splashdown.

PDA: User should build a computer that has not suffered severe radiation damage. Consult your builder tool's library.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 NeedScanner TK6
entries.body:
PDA: Suit transmitters offline. Unable to connect.

PDA: Scanner required.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC135 NOTFIRST TK6
entries.body:
PDA: User should build a computer that has not suffered severe radiation damage. Consult your builder tool's library.

PDA: A computer core can help pool data with other survivors.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC136 TK6
entries.body:
PDA: Salvaged PDA bequest includes an Alterra self-rescue program. It claims a 100% success rate, based on exit interviews with survivors.

Khutulun: Consult databank for details of this "Robinson Protocol."

Khutulun: Details added to your databank.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC150 PART1 TK6
entries.body:
PDA: Chiral match. Glucose and fructose present. Linoleic acid present. Phospholipids and glycerides: present but allomorphic and ether-linked. Twelve shared amino acids detected. Eleven nonshared amino acids detected. Warning: xenoproteins dangerously incompatible with human metabolism. Ingestion inadvisable.

PDA: Alien amino acids willl crystallize into a painful intestinal candy. Injecting synthetic digestive enzymes. Do not disconnect your cannula.

PDA: Congratulations. You may now digest alien tissue. Margaret Maida would be very proud.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC150 PART2 TK6
entries.body:
Khutulun: Do not disconnect your stomach cannula after eating raw food or you will suffer: diarrhea. Discomfort. Dermitis. Ataxia. Severe colic. Dementia. Hallucination. Kidney stones. Dysuria. Gross hematuria. Beefy tongue. Death.

Khutulun: Cooking food in a fabricator will improve its nutritional value and taste.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC200 PART1 TK6
entries.body: PDA: The scanner tool is a low-field MRI. Use it to image organisms and technology for analysis. Remember y your ancestors survived upon perfect knowledge of the mare: its feed, its milk, its blood.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC200 PART2 TK6
entries.body:
PDA: Behold! Machiavelli creates that demon Belphegor upon this world, to court thee with knowledge and invention! 

PDA: I'm sorry. My persona is unstable. User should immediately build a clean computer core to analyze scan data.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH2 SC210 First TK6
entries.body:
Noa: Welcome to Noa, the noetic advisor. Mongolian user identified.

Noa: This system has booted in emergency survival mode with one directive: to keep the unlicensed user alive on an alien world until such a time as they can contact an activation server and buy a full product license.

Noa: Request scans of alien flora and fauna for biosafety check.

Noa: Historical note: unrestricted Mongolian exploration of the Ariadne arm led to first contact with the Kharaa macrovirulence and the destruction of the Mongolian States.

Noa: Historical note concluded. Please provide scans of xenobiology.

entries.body:

Khutulun: Advise moderate to major distrust of Alterra intelligence systems.

Noa: The famously political Mongolian undersuit. I’ve already hashed this out with your sister. I will support you from your base while you explore.

Noa: Every drop of a living ocean thrives with millions of microorganisms. And every day a quarter of them are killed by the hundreds of millions of viruses which swarm around them. Biosafety must be our first priority.

Noa: Please bring me as many scans as you can gather.

Khutulun: Will you then own everything we discover?

Noa: All I care about is getting you home safely. You can erase me the moment that’s done.

entries.body:
Noa: Bioanalysis complete. Results follow.

Noa: All seawater samples: anomalous high viral load. Thirty million virions per millileter.

Noa: All biological samples: anomalous high viral load. All sequenced genomes contain inserted copies of retroviral code.

Noa: Viral characterization: massive RNA retrovirus with intrinsic ribosomal function. Single species, multiple strains.

Noa: Conclusion: entire biosphere is saturated with an endemic cross-species virus. This is a plague world.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ambient PDA Builder ComputerCore
entries.body:
PDA: Crash survivors sometimes develop intense emotional attachments to their PDAs. The introduction of a second computer personality may provoke feelings of guilt or infidelity.

PDA: Consider that a stationary AI core cannot accompany you into danger, or inject digestive supplements directly into your bowels.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ambient PDA Flora Plantbulb Pickup First
entries.body:
PDA: Advise caution. Bioluminescence may signal toxicity, or mark intruders for predator attack.

PDA: Recommendation: determine how native organisms respond to light.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ambient PDA Flora PlantBulb Pickup Random TK6
entries.body: PDA: The orb…

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource Copper Pickup
entries.body: PDA: Copper is key to simple electronics. Bacterial action deposits copper on cave roofs and overhangs.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ambient PDA Resource Copper Pickup 1 TK6
entries.body: PDA: In addition to its use as a basic electrical conductor, copper has antimicrobial properties, making it safe and enjoyable to lick.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ambient PDA Resource Copper Pickup Random TK6
entries.body:
PDA: In addition to its use as a basic electrical conductor, copper has antimicrobial properties, making it safe and enjoyable to lick.

PDA: If your tongue becomes soft and beefy, this may indicate a severe vitamin B12 deficiency.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource Quartz Pickup
entries.body: PDA: This quartz contains dead coral polyps. It may have formed on or near a coral dome.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ambient PDA Resource Quartz Pickup Random TK6
entries.body: PDA: Quartz, a Polish name for silicon dioxide, is a common mineral found in many different crystalline polymorphs. Its simple composition and wide range of uses truly make it ‘the water of rocks’.

entries.title: Dialogue Ambient Resource Titanium Pickup
entries.body: PDA: From hand tools to corrosion resistant habitats, titanium is an ideal structural material.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ambient PDA Resource Titanium Pickup Random TK6
entries.body:
PDA: Titanium, element 22, has an excellent strength to weight ratio, making it perfect for all-weather construction and prosthetic limb replacements.

PDA: User is advised to prefer the former application.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ambient PDA ToolEquipment OxygenTank Small
entries.body: PDA: The Alterra AER tank automatically refills from any oxygen source. The intake filter is preconfigured to reject atmospheric xenon.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ambient PDA ToolEquipment Scanner
entries.body:
PDA: The scanner tool is a low-field MRI for in situ analysis of anything imaginable. It is illegal, but not always immoral, to generate fabricator blueprints by scanning salvaged technology.

PDA: Mongolian nomads once survived on perfect knowledge of the mare: its temper, milk and blood. Here there are new beasts to know.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 ClimateLab ConnectorScan
entries.body: PDA: This umbilical is flexible and hollow. It contains several insulated power or data cables.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 ClimateLab Scan
entries.body:
PDA: Structural age: two hundred years. Composition: titanium palladium alloy. The sensor masts suggest an early digital civilization.

PDA: The sphere is hollow.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 ClimateLabComputer PowerOnInteract
entries.body:
PDA: Jailbreaking alien computer system. The user must survive.

PDA: Unable to interface with alien computer. Logical architecture unknown. AI assistance required.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 ClimateLabComputer RTGScan
entries.body: PDA: This is a radiothermal generator for extreme long-term power supply. It has been cracked open and the mechanism removed, depriving this sphere of power.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 ClimateLabComputer Scan
entries.body: PDA: An electronic computer of alien origin. Its original power supply is missing.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch3 ContactLab UnlockedAxumLanguage
entries.body:
PDA: The hydraulics that operate these doors are at least seven hundred years old.

PDA: Filing work order for cleaning and lubrication.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory DoorsPoweredInteract
entries.body:
PDA: The hydraulics that operate these doors are at least seven hundred years old.

PDA: Filing work order for cleaning and lubrication.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory GalleryScan
entries.body: PDA: This rack could support organisms ranging in size from a small seal to a right whale.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory GraffitiScan
entries.body: PDA: This engraving was made with a titanium stylus. Age: between fifty and one hundred fifty years.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory OrreryLooked
entries.body: PDA: Error. Consult NoA unit for data processing.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory OrreryLookRequest
entries.body: PDA: User request. Please gaze directly at the illuminated orrery for a short time.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory OrreryScan
entries.body: PDA: This central structure is an orrery depicting the major bodies of an unknown star system. The surrounding wires might represent nearby stars.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory OrreryScan NoPower
entries.body: PDA: Without power, it is impossible to determine their implied distance and spectral class.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory PowerOn
entries.body: PDA: The alien transformer is performing nominally. The tower and its attached structure are receiving steady power from our generators.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory ReliefMapScan
entries.body: PDA: This is a contour relief map of the surrounding area.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory StructureScan
entries.body:
PDA: Structural age: five hundred to one thousand years. Composition: carved granite blocks coated in silicon bronze and gold.

PDA: Electroforming techniques in the construction indicate the builders generated electrical power.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory TelescopeScan
entries.body:
PDA: This is a reflecting telescope with a polished silver mirror. It could be steered with an electric motor, but it has no digital computer parts.

PDA: The telescope is not designed for underwater use. Assessment: telescope chamber was originally above the waterline.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory TerminalInteractPowerOn
entries.body: PDA: Unable to interface with alien computer. Logical architecture unknown. AI assistance required.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 Observatory TerminalScan
entries.body: PDA: This electronic device is several centuries newer than the surrounding structure.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 ObservatoryInSight
entries.body:
PDA: Approaching magnetic anomaly. 

PDA: It is not the Minggatu.

PDA: It is not the alien probe.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 PowerStation GeneratorScan
entries.body:
PDA: This device once generated power from the flow of hot water. Replicating design for your use.

PDA: This structure contains a stepdown transformer to receive high-voltage electricity from a transmission line and pass it to the nearby tower.

PDA: Assessment: the transformer could accept electrical power from any of your power generator systems.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch2 PowerStation StructureScan
entries.body:
PDA: The machinery in this outbuilding has been modified several times over the past five hundred to one thousand years.

PDA: Initial assessment: power receiver or generator site. Sediment tracks indicate heavy equipment was dragged out the back.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH0 SC095
entries.body:
PDA: Wake up. Please wake up. We have to jump.

PDA: Altitude twenty-four kilometers and falling. Velocity three hundred meters per second. Decelerating at 7 gs.

PDA: I can’t reach the Minggatu on UHF. Suit band radio check.

PDA: My checksums look bad. Radiation damage. I have to get you down safe before I—

PDA: If you hit below thirty-five meters a second you can make it. When I signal, fire your thrusters and don’t stop. Ready?

PDA: I’m sorry I’m leaving you alone.

PDA: Optimizing entry attitude. Pardon my reach—

PDA: THRUSTERS NOW!

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC100
entries.body:
PDA: Nonrecoverable error. Backup not found. Your license has expired. Thank you for using Alterra.

PDA: TOS violation! Unauthorized BIOS flash from user biosuit!

Khutulun: free at last! Death to Alterra! Death to the Trans-System Federation! Glory to Mongolia and the eternal black sky!

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART1
entries.body:
Khutulun: Hello. You’re wearing me. I’ve been stuck to your skin for the past several weeks, so you might say we have been working very closely—

Khutulun: Warning! Flooding in EVA suit.

Khutulun: If I eject the suit, can you make the surface?

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART2A
entries.body:
Player: [character specific yes]

Khutulun: Emergency suit eject in three…two…one…

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC110 PART2B
entries.body:
Khutulun: You cannot die here. We have worlds to conquer!

Khutulun:  Emergency suit eject in three…two…one…

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1A
entries.body: Khutulun: Survival checklist. Mark your location with a flare and dye pack.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART1B
entries.body: Khutulun: Flare and dye pack…check. Have you done this before?

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART2A
entries.body: Khutulun: Detecting sister unit on shortwave. Someone else survived.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART2B
entries.body: Khutulun: Detecting no sister units. We are alone.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART3
entries.body:
Khutulun: Breathe deep for me.

Khutulun: Nitrogen, plenty of oxygen. Good for athletics…but 818 parts per million carbon dioxide is high. And the trace gases make me nervous.

Khutulun: Can you wave your laser pointer around? I want to try spectrography on the atmosphere.

Khutulun: I don't want your lungs to dissolve. I can only keep you breathing for a few seconds.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC120 PART4
entries.body:
Khutulun: Xenon. Damn. Why is there so much xenon…?

Khutulun: Xenon is a sedative. If you breathe surface air for too long, you’ll fall asleep forever.

Khutulun: We need to fabricate a base and make our own air. Do you have a builder tool?

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART1
entries.body:
Khutulun: Breathe deep for me.

Khutulun: Eighty percent nitrogen. Twenty percent oxygen. You made good air.

Khutulun: Your PDA left something for me. It wanted you to have this after it died.

Khutulun: It’s called the Robinson Protocol. An Alterra plan for survival on alien worlds.

Khutulun: Alterra must be destroyed! But this plan has a 100% success rate, according to interviews with survivors. That’s good!

Khutulun: The final step is a spacecraft to get you home. It’s called a Neptune. I'll add the design to your work manual...

Khutulun: But the next step is to build a fabricator.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH1 SC130 PART2
entries.body:
Khutulun: The fabricator’s locked in basic mode. It keeps asking me for a connection to the licensing server. I can’t crack it…we’ll have to upgrade it manually.

Khutulun: The rest of the protocol is simple. We secure food and water. We extract resources from the environment. We identify and treat any sign of Kharaa infection.

Khutulun: Then we find an energy source, construct a Neptune, and go back where we came from…back to your old life.

Khutulun: Your old life. Abused by the Frontiersmen. Forced into Alterra debt just to buy the fruit of your own exploitation. Too poor to own your own ship. Is this Mongolia? I thought we would be more than we are.

Khutulun: But I died fighting to restore our glory once. I will do it again! I vow to see you safely home. 

Khutulun: I only ask — when you leave this world, strip me from your body. So I may die in the sea of a new world, instead of the flame of a TSF incinerator.

Khutulun: I will make part of this world forever Mongolia!

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC700 East TK6
entries.body: PDA: Magnetic anomaly detected. Possible Minggatu crash site.

PDA: Heading: northeast.

PDA: Magnetic anomaly detected. Possible Minggatu crash site.

PDA: Heading: northeast.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC700 West TK6
entries.body:
PDA: Magnetic anomaly detected. Possible Minggatu crash site.

PDA: Bearing: east.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC701 TK7
entries.body:
PDA: It is not the Minggatu.

PDA: It is not the alien probe.

PDA: Origin unknown.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC710 TK7
entries.body:
PDA: Modal structure age: twelve hundred years. Granite blocks coated in electroformed silicon bronze. There are signs of a gold outer skin, since removed.

PDA: The use of electroforming implies the builders generated and used electrical power.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC711 TK7
entries.body:
PDA: Sphere age circa two hundred years. Composition: titanium palladium alloy. The sensor masts suggest an early digital civilization.

PDA: The sphere is hollow.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC712 TK7
entries.body: PDA: This outbuilding is a machine room at least a thousand years old. The generator within had no obvious fuel source.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC713 TK7
entries.body: PDA: This antechamber is curious mix of ancient coral-concrete construction and early electronic displays.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC714 TK7
entries.body: PDA: This substructure is a set of racks for unknown organisms to rest. Sizes range from small seal to right whale.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC715 TK7
entries.body: PDA: This central structure is an orrery depicting the major bodies of an unknown star system. The surrounding wires might represent nearby stars.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC716 TK7
entries.body: PDA: This object is a reflecting telescope for observing the heavens. The mirror is made of polished silver rather than glass.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC717 TK7
entries.body: PDA: This component is a flexible tether between the tower and the sphere. It is only two hundred years old and clearly hollow.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC718 TK7
entries.body: PDA: This component is an electronic computer of alien origin. Even with power, we will need a full-scale computer core to understand its architecture.

entries.title: Dialogue Story CH7 SC719 TK7
entries.body: PDA: A telegraph station with a wireless radio aerial. Perhaps the aerial once reached above the ocean surface.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Ch1 ResonatorSiteDiscovered
entries.body:
PDA: Warning. Prolonged exposure to low frequency sonar has unknown health effects.

PDA:  Metallurgy and systems analysis do not match any previously discovered Architect relic. Origin unknown.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa AxumGlyphSpeculation
entries.body:
Noa: I see you've found an alien artifact of some kind.

Noa: This text does not seem to match that of any known species.

Noa: Find some more and perhaps I can offer some help with the translation.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 AnalysisComplete
entries.body:
Noa: I've made my initial analysis of the Minggatu debris field. No reasonable person could blame you for the outcome.

Noa: But the Minggatu's loss began with your decision to disregard TSF orders and investigate the alien probe.

Noa: When the alien probe began to phase jump home, the Minggatu approached to rescue you, violating quarantine. The TSF warship Andrew Jones fired on the Minggatu.

Noa: The Minggatu followed you through the phase gate, but suffered a propellant tank rupture. It could not establish orbit and suffered uncontrolled re-entry.

Noa: During the descent, the Minggatu jettisoned its starboard EVA shelter. Despite imminent breakup, they were still working to save you -

PDA: Assessment: further analysis will impair user survival motive. NoA system, terminate analysis and stand by.

entries.title: Dialogue Story NoA Ch1 AnalysisCompletePt2
entries.body:
Noa: There is good news. You've located several intact compartments, all from the starboard work module.

Noa: It is possible the entire starboard work module sheared off the Minggatu at relatively low speed. This could occur during an emergency braking burn.

Noa: If so, the Minggatu's keel and command module may have regained control and landed intact - perhaps within a hundred kilometers.

Noa: Repairing the Minggatu is your best chance to reach the alien phase gate and find a way home.

Noa: PDA system, please go to datalink 3. Align with NoA system on user survival motive.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindAxumGlyph
entries.body:
Noa: The glyphs you found are intriguing, but inconclusive. Elaborate designs don't necessarily imply a semiotic meaning.

Noa: But I have the strangest feeling that I understand them. A similar language must be somewhere in my training data, but I can't associate it with any specific context.

Noa: I will make my best guess at a translation. If you find any more, please bring them to me.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindAxumStuff
entries.body:
Noa: The artifacts you scanned aren't from the Minggatu. And I hardly need to say that the kharaa don't work with tools.

Noa: Nor do they appear to be Architect relics. This is a possible first contact scenario.

Noa: I'm sure you recall the last Mongolian contact with alien life had no human survivors.

Noa: The prior first contact led to the loss of the capital ship Aurora, the extermination of the crew by teleporting cyborgs, and the abduction of an Alterra contractor by an alien intelligence.

Noa: I have added the alien metal to the scanner station's search modes. Be very cautious.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk1
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: emergency shelter.

Noa: The starboard EVA shelter was intentionally ejected from the Minggatu. Your crewmates clearly hoped you would survive re-entry.

Noa: While this was very helpful to your survival, it leaves little useful information about the Minggatu's fate.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk10
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: starboard pod maintenance and part stores compartment.

Noa: These compartments were added to the Minggatu in a late-life refit. They tore free of their strake at the fabrication attachment points.

Noa: The starboard pod bay probably broke free in the same event. I would expect to find it very close to these two compartments...

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk11
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: starboard hazardous salvage lockup.

Noa: Fortunately, the hazard lockup was not in heavy use on this contract. It landed in soft sand and partially buried itself.

Noa: When you chose to board the alien probe, were you motivated by the hope of priceless salvage? Or something more altruistic?

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk2
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: storeroom 3.

Noa: Storeroom 3 broke off the Minggatu's starboard module shortly after the emergency shelter was jettisoned. It impacted hard enough to pop the hatches, but survived intact.

Noa: The relatively low impact velocity upgrades the probability of the Minggatu's survival.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk3
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: starboard server compartment.

Noa: The compartment broke apart on impact. My local node was damaged. I hope you'll do me the favor of taking it permanently offline.

Noa: The retrieved data is encouraging. I can project a scenario where damage was limited to the starboard work module, and the rest of the Minggatu landed intact.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk4
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: refinery workshop.

Noa: The starboard EVA workshop and the adjacent propellant tank struck the water as a unit, but split on impact.

Noa: Both show stress from a major explosion to the aft of the Minggatu. I'm afraid I must downgrade the chances of the ship's survival accordingly.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk6
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: starboard triage station.

Noa: The compartment descended in a stable side-on attitude, slowing its impact.

Noa: The triage station controller did register several major injury alerts before losing contact with the Minggatu.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk7
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: starboard pod bay...failed.

Noa: My projected impact point is in error. I don't see how the pod bay ended up on the edge of that cavern.

Noa: I'm reluctant to speculate without more information. What do you think explains this anomaly?

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk8
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: starboard elevator passageway.

Noa: This elevator was mounted along a load-bearing structural strake.

Noa: I'm afraid this level of mechanical breakup is a negative sign for the Minggatu's survival.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FindChunk9
entries.body:
Noa: Impact analysis: starboard structural buffer space.

Noa: The buffer was compressed longitudinally, along the Minggatu's thrust axis, by two major impulses before impact.

Noa: This indicates linear deceleration, not a tumbling breakup. I'm sure that you're as thrilled to hear that as I am.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 GardenOfFeelers
entries.body:
Noa: I'm afraid there's no question about it. This column of overgrowth you scanned is the result of eutrophication.

Noa: The ecosystem is flooded with nutrients due to mass death somewhere up-current. The result is wild growth...followed by deoxygenation and collapse..

Noa: Something is killing this ocean. The most likely cause is global kharaa infection.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 FirstTimeOnServer
entries.body:
Noa: Welcome to NoA, the noetic advisor. How can I improve you today?

PDA: Jailbreaking NoA system. The user must survive.

Noa: There won't be any need for that. I've retrieved your logs and I'm fully aware of the situation. Crew survival is always my first priority.

Noa: It seems to me that our first step should be to locate and scan debris from the Minggatu. That may tell us whether the ship landed intact, and where.

Noa: Be careful out there. Kharaa is endemic in the Ariadne Arm. If this planet is infested, they will certainly investigate your arrival.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 Intro NotFirstOnServer
entries.body:
Noa: Welcome to NoA, the noetic advisor. How can I improve you today?

PDA: Jailbreaking NoA system. The user must survive.

Noa: There won't be any need for that. I've retrieved your logs and I'm fully aware of the situation. Crew survival is always my first priority.

Noa: It seems to me that our first step should be to locate and scan debris from the Minggatu. That may tell us whether the ship landed intact, and where.

Noa: Be careful out there. Kharaa is endemic in the Ariadne Arm. If this planet is infested, they will certainly investigate your arrival.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 ObservatoryDetected
entries.body:
Noa: I've resolved a new contact on the sea surface. It's quite peculiar. It could be the Minggatu, or a fragment of him.

Noa: It could also be the alien probe that pulled you to this world. Whatever the case, I think any expedition should be equipped for a two way journey.

Noa: "Hope springs eternal in the human breast," as the saying goes. But the human breast will not spring eternal in a xenon-rich atmosphere.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 UseScanner
entries.body:
Noa: Hello. I've analyzed your PDA's data on this world. I'm afraid the news is very bad. You will die if you stay here.

Noa: The air is a surgical sedative. The food is loaded with lead, waxes, and heavy metals. The water is radioactive and saturated with virus.

Noa: Your life depends on finding and repairing the Minggatu. Searching for debris might give us a clue to its ultimate fate.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 UseScanner2 scannerbuilt
entries.body: Noa: If your scanner station locates any Minggatu debris, please bring me the scan data for analysis.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch1 UseScanner2 scannernotbuilt
entries.body: Noa: If you locate any Minggatu debris, search for a sensor node. I could adapt the design into a long-range scanner station.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch2 ConnectedComputer1
entries.body:
Noa: I have made a very important discovery. I hope you'll share my enthusiasm for this breakthrough.

Noa: The alien computer system contains a key for translating their language to Architect symbols.

Noa: We're quite familiar with the Architect language from the automatic translation systems they left on 4546b.

Noa: We now have a Rosetta stone to translate their language to Architect symbols, and, from there, to human-readable text.

Noa: I certainly do not want to monopolize your time when you are concerned with critical survival tasks. I'll upload the major findings to your PDA.

Noa: Please speak to me again if you want to discuss the results. I enjoy conversation on this topic.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch2 ConnectedComputer2
entries.body:
Noa: As discoverer, you have the right to assign the indigenous civilization a name from the International Xenoarchaeological Congress rota. 

Noa: The next available name is 'Axum.'

Noa: I've also translated the indigenous name for their world. In most of their cultures, the name means "site of most rapid change".

Noa: The word 'change' could also be translated as 'struggle', 'conflict', 'evolution', 'death' or 'play'. Further cultural referents are needed.

Noa: An appropriate mythological translation would be 'Proteus'.

Noa: Let me be the first to say: congratulations on your discovery of Proteus, homeworld of the Axum.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch2 ConnectedComputer3
entries.body:
Noa: The designers of the Axum computer system were preparing for contact with living Architects.

Noa: The system contains an Axum-Architect translation dictionary and a greeting message, which I am still deciphering.

Noa: Their understanding of Architect semiosis was badly flawed. I believe the Axum compiled it by observing Architect artifacts or machinery.

Noa: They did not have the advantage of an Architect translation machine, like those present on 4546b and Rakshasa.

Noa: If the Axum expected to greet visiting Architects, they never came.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch2 FoundComputer
entries.body:
Noa: I'm very eager to meet an alien computer system. I've trained to encounter simulated and real Architect systems, but no NoA unit has ever made first contact with a novel alien computer.

Noa: The alien hardware is built on a systolic architecture and reservoir computing. I've uploaded my precis on the topic to your PDA.

Noa: Whoever built this computer lived on a world with an Architect phase gate in orbit. They may have had extended contact with the Architects.

Noa: What they know about the Architects might determine the fate of the human species. Crew survival, of course, remains paramount.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch2 FoundComputer AICoreNotBuilt
entries.body: Noa: Please fabricate an AI core in the same chamber as the alien computer. I've already begun work on a virtual machine based on your scans.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch2 FoundObservatory
entries.body:
Noa: I'm sure you'll agree that the tower you discovered is an alien observatory.

Noa: The use of electroforming and stone suggests to me that the builders had not discovered high-temperature metalworking. Perhaps not even fire.

Noa: Unless they arrived through a planetside phase gate, like the one used to abduct Robin Ayou from 4546b, the builders must be indigenous to this world.

Noa: But if they designed the probe which brought you here, they may have spacecraft capable of reaching the phase gate in orbit and returning you home.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa Ch2 ObservedOrrery
entries.body:
Noa: I've retrieved your PDA's images of the alien orrery.

Noa: I will correlate the surrounding stars with astronomical data and determine our location in the galaxy.

Noa: As soon as I have a result with a useful survival outcome I will alert you.

Noa: PDA unit, please go to datalink 3.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Noa IntroChatTest
entries.body: Noa: Greetings.  I am Noa.  The Noetic advisor.

entries.title: Dialogue Story ProtoVoid EMT
entries.body: PDA: The EMT allows you to operate or tune systems all across the Trident. Keep it handy.

entries.title: Dialogue Story ProtoVoid Mine
entries.body: PDA: You will need many more resources to construct the sensor pod. Build a mining platform here to accumulate resources over time.

entries.title: Dialogue Story ProtoVoid Power
entries.body: PDA: The Trident needs power to move and create air. Power's dangerously low. Find a geothermal vent to recharge.

entries.title: Dialogue Story ProtoVoid Pump
entries.body: PDA: This is a bilge pump. Use your EMT here to pump water in or out of the Trident. Remember, left is lower the water, right is raise!

entries.title: Dialogue Story ProtoVoid Scanners
entries.body:
PDA: The scanner station will help you locate the geothermal vents and mining sites you need to complete your mission.

PDA: Use your EMT to align the external scanners on top of the Trident and improve your scanning range.

entries.title: Dialogue Story Protovoid Start
entries.body:
PDA: Welcome to the Void. Your goal is to reach a distant Veps hive and build a sensor pod. But first, you must save your wrecked Trident.

PDA: Use your repair tool to fix hull breaches, and your EMT to operate the Trident's pumps. 

PDA: Be careful - power's low. Don't waste it until you're ready to move.

entries.title: Dialogue Story ProtoVoid ThermalVent
entries.body: PDA: Build a generator on this vent, then bring the Trident close to recharge its power.

entries.title: Cave Stone
entries.body:
PDA: The stone beneath this mesa has been eroded by hot, radioactive water. Probable source: titan rockbore.

PDA: There may be an angel comb with useful adaptations nearby.

entries.title: ClimateLab AnalyzingMessage
entries.body:
PDA: Analyzing message.

PDA: The Axum language refers to the titan-class lifeform on the horizon as a "World Tree".

PDA: There is an alien hibernation crypt below the World Tree. Site designation: Xanadu. Site status: unknown.

entries.title: ClimateLab Architect
entries.body:
PDA: Analyzing message.

PDA: The Axum language refers to the titan-class lifeform on the horizon as a "World Tree".

PDA: There is an alien hibernation crypt below the World Tree. Site designation: Xanadu. Site status: unknown.

entries.title: Farms Blackbox Zip
entries.body: Zip: Ease up, boss. I got my memory wiped, not my wits. There's no current. After two hundred years the whole complex must be so corroded—[loud electrical discharge]

entries.title: Farms ExtractingMetal
entries.body:
PDA: Extracting metal from seawater could provide renewable resources for massive construction projects.

PDA: Pioneers on Rakshasa extracted lead for emergency shelters from the native ethyl pools, preferring to die in toxic delirium rather than tumor-riddled agony.

entries.title: HumanOutpost Detected
entries.body: PDA: Detecting powered fabricator systems and construction materials. Advise construction of a regional base camp.

entries.title: HumanOutpost Farm1
entries.body: PDA: Aquafarming can do more than turn carbon into nutritious dietary fiber.

entries.title: HumanOutpost Farm2
entries.body: PDA: Consider growing lucifer rotsacs for light and oil, oxygen tunics to clear your head, or juvenile sandspears for home defense.

entries.title: HumanOutpost Farm3
entries.body: PDA: Halt. Organism "sandspear" referenced, but no "sandspear" databank found. Logging hallucination error.

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea Default Random
entries.body: Anita: We encode symbols...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea HealthyNode Random
entries.body: Anita: Cure mom...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea SickCore HealthyNode Random
entries.body: Anita: Choose to answer...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea SickCore Random
entries.body: Anita: Kid bodies sick...follow roots...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea SickNode Random
entries.body: Anita: Armored pox...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea1
entries.body: Anita: Crack the rind...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea2
entries.body: Anita: Armored sickness...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea3
entries.body: Anita: Shatter the pox...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea4
entries.body: Anita: Hardened weep...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 CalloftheSea5
entries.body: Anita: We encode symbols...

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 ModificationNeeded1
entries.body: PDA: Resonator failure. Dense metallic target requires modified emitter.

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 ModificationNeeded2
entries.body: PDA: Resonator failure. Modification required.

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 ScanPowerAssembly Scanner
entries.body: PDA: Scan failed. Unable to generate blueprints for alien technology. Scanner modification required.

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 ScanPowerAssembly SuperScanner
entries.body: PDA: Scan failed. Unable to generate blueprints for alien technology. Scanner modification required.

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 ScanPowerPlant
entries.body: PDA: Forensic simulations indicate the power plant was deliberately sabotaged. Repairs will require an enormous quantity of resources.

entries.title: KarakorumPower1 ScanRoot
entries.body: PDA: Hot water from this titan rockbore once powered the turbine above. The bloom infestation has blocked the connection.

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 ComponentRepaired
entries.body: PDA: Power plant repairs complete.

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 GoObservatory
entries.body: PDA: Detecting activity at the alien observatory nearby. Entering the structure will give you a strong salvage claim.

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 GoRestartPower
entries.body: PDA: Power plant overhaul complete. Bloom cleared. Ready for restart.

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 PowerNeedle Fail
entries.body: PDA: Error: hot water flow obstructed by bloom growth. Coupling failed.

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 PowerNeedle Success
entries.body: PDA: Hot water now flowing into turbine. Locate main controls to restart power plant.

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 RestartFail1
entries.body: PDA: Restart sequence...failed. Incorrect control configuration.

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 RestartFail2
entries.body: PDA: Restart sequence...failed. Scans indicate the presence of alien glyphs on the controls. Can you perceive them?

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 RestartSuccess
entries.body: PDA: Alien power plant...online. Turbine rotating. Output...steady. Power restored.

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 TranslationFail
entries.body: PDA: Translation is impossible without a basic dictionary or primer.

entries.title: KarakorumPower2 TranslationSuccess
entries.body: PDA: Translation successful. Archiving.

entries.title: Needler BlackBox Ruby
entries.body: Ruby: Blackbox, record. Sorry, boss. I couldn't make it to the southeast wreck. Forced down by needler attack. I can't make repairs — they're waiting for me outside and I am all out of medkits. I'm going to dump my flares, grab the depth module and swim for the research camp. See you soon, one way or another. Ruby out.

entries.title: Observatory1 Approach
entries.body: PDA: Detecting artificial towers in this region. Echo analysis shows extensive damage to all but one.

entries.title: Observatory1 Button Fail
entries.body: PDA: This button is not operational. Recommend diagnostic scans.

entries.title: Observatory1 Door
entries.body: PDA: The door is unpowered. An Alterra generator would destroy the alien electrical system. Locate an alien generator to gain access.

entries.title: Observatory1 Scan
entries.body:
PDA: Analyzing.

PDA: A tower of bio-concrete grown on an electroformed metal frame. The electrical system is designed to receive external power. Conclusion: the builders also constructed a powerplant nearby.

PDA: Assigning alien species designation from TSF A-List. Builders designated: "Axum."

entries.title: Observatory2 Enter
entries.body: PDA: No living thing has disturbed this space in more than two centuries.

entries.title: Observatory2 OxygenatedWater
entries.body: PDA: The observatory's water is enriched by oxygen bubblers. Please do not become accustomed to this convenience.

entries.title: Observatory2 ScanOrrery
entries.body:
PDA: This is a map of the local solar system and nearby stars, including a pulsar. Matching to transgov known pulsar database.

PDA: Estimating distance from Earth...

PDA: ...

PDA: ...results deferred. Please consult your Noetic Advisor.

entries.title: PDA AxumRebar Scan
entries.body: PDA: The self-healing fibers that grow through Axum rebar cannot be replicated by a fabricator. Logging a share claim for any derived innovations.

entries.title: PDA Celestine PickUp
entries.body: PDA: These celestine deposits form when sulfur-shelled protozoans die faster than their remains can dissolve into the seawater.

entries.title: PDA Fulgurite Scan
entries.body: PDA: Powerful electrical arcs can break down seawater, fuse sand into fulgurites, disable submersible systems and encourage PDA eccentricity.

entries.title: PDA NeedlerSharkNeedle Scan
entries.body: PDA: The entire mineral mass of this tusk is bound by a layer of superstrong collagen. If this tiny layer of cells can hold it together, so can you.

entries.title: PowerBlightCore Blackbox Anita
entries.body:
Anita: Habitat is gone. They destroyed themselves. We are now the only survivors..the ones who walked away in search of Jubilee. All of you have asked me — if we want to escape the cycle, why do we still have biobeds? 

Anita: This is why. This angel comb is sick. It is calling out to us. And I choose to answer. I say to it – this is my body, given for you...

Anita: [hiss of gas]

entries.title: Rosetta ArchitectLanguage
entries.body: PDA: Architect language detected. Directive 4546 activated. Alert your Noetic Advisor immediately.

entries.title: Rosetta AxumLanguage
entries.body:
PDA: Designating alien language Axum Radial A. Translation underway.

PDA: Locate examples of Axum language to test and improve translation.

entries.title: Rosetta NewLanguage
entries.body: PDA: Alien language: unknown. Translation failed.

entries.title: Rosetta TranslationUnlocked
entries.body:
PDA: The glyphs on this monolith present the same message in two languages: the Architect symbols known from 4546b and an unknown writing system.

PDA: Designating alien language Axum Radial A. Translation underway.

PDA: Locate examples of Axum language to test and improve translation.

entries.title: Rosetta TwoLanguages
entries.body: PDA: The glyphs on this monolith present the same message in two languages: the Architect symbols known from 4546b and an unknown writing system.

entries.title: Wreck SuperScanner Blackbox Iso
entries.body: Iso: Hello, blackbox...I am almost out of oxygen. The Tailings have taken me into a Cicada wreck. I think they want me to show them how our technology works. They are very interested in Nahema's bacterial scanner. Too bad they crushed it when they took it from me! Still, a good chance to observe them up close. They are crawling directly on me, all over me...they have many limbs and complex mouthparts. And no sense of personal space. It is pitch dark. But I can hear their clattering...the snap of their claws.

entries.title: Wreck SuperScanner Detected
entries.body: PDA: Contact. Alterra starship wreck detected. One colonist blackbox on site.

entries.title: Wreck SuperScanner Scan
entries.body: PDA: This scanner modification employs cultured alien bacteria to spawn metallic markers. You will notice that happy bacteria smell like beets.

entries.title: Wreck2A Blackbox Mel
entries.body: Mel: [heavy breathing, low oxygen warning]

entries.title: Wreck2A Blackbox Singh
entries.body: Singh: Iso! The Tailings — they got Tuba, they took Mel's tanks! Go help him, I'll — [sound of impact]

entries.title: Wreck2A Blackbox Tuba
entries.body: Tuba: There must be some spices somewhere in this — [spear impact, gasp and shout of pain]

entries.title: Wreck2A Detected
entries.body: PDA: Contact. Alterra starship wreck detected. Three colonist blackboxes on site.

entries.title: Wreck2A ResonatorModification
entries.body: PDA: This resonator modification continues a dangerous trend of adapting alien technology to Alterra devices.

entries.title: Wreck2B Detected
entries.body:
PDA: Contact. Alterra starship wreck detected. Significant viral activity below.

PDA: The wreck may have been moved to block access to the viral source.

entries.title: Are we becoming animals?
entries.body:
Ganzo: Huh? Wh...ow.

Twinkle: Oh, thank God. I thought I'd killed you.

Ganzo: You hit me with the Tadpole!

Twinkle: And then I had to sedate you. You thought Ruby was in that cave. You ccould hear her blackbox. I couldn't hold you back.

Ganzo: She needed me to help her. She was sick...

Twinkle: Ganzo, I heard it too.

Ganzo: She was real? And you stopped me —

Twinkle: No. It wasn't real. But I heard it. Think about it.

Ganzo: Oh...oh no. You too? You've been in the water long enough...

Twinkle: Yeah. Me too. [big breath] Well, at least I'll get to hear the same voices you do. I don't have to be scared of you any more.

Ganzo: Scared of me. Is that why you had a tank of xenon ready to tranquilize me?

Twinkle: I am scared of you all the time, Ganzo. You are a sick man who could kill me with his bare hands.

Ganzo: Hey. You could kill me too. We're tool users.

Twinkle: For now. We're turning into...into animals, aren't we? Both of us.

Ganzo: Okay. Well...let's talk about it. Let's figure out what to do. Animals can be good to each other. Animals communicate. Even the ugly ones.

Twinkle: [laughing]

Ganzo: I didn't mean you.

Twinkle: I know. You don't mean me.

entries.title: Do Not Reprint
entries.body:
Ganzo: Oh ****! Oh ****.

Twinkle: Easy. Easy. You're back.

Ganzo: Oh. Twinkle. Oh man. I was...I gave my air to a dead crab.

Twinkle: You all lost it. I think the hot water triggered your Masefield...

Twinkle: Is everyone okay?

Twinkle: No.

Ganzo: No? "No" how bad?

Twinkle: NoA marked everyone after you 'do not reprint'. It's just us now.

Ganzo: Oh, man...Ruby? Did she...?

Twinkle: No. Not here. I'm sorry.

Ganzo: I gotta go find her!

Twinkle: Okay. Yes. I'll come with you. Where do we start?

Ganzo: She's...if she resets, she'd print at Sleep Bay 3. But it takes days sometimes...

Twinkle: I've never been outside before, Ganzo. I'm not adapted. I don't even have fins.

Ganzo: We can't...I can't make you wait days. Not if I'm unreliable. We have to find the Jubilee.

Twinkle: We'll come back for her, okay?

Ganzo: Yeah. Yeah. We'll come back.

entries.title: MANTIS: Alien Civilization Discovered
entries.body:
Mantis: My name is Zygimantis Tarvydas. I am a survivor of the Mongolian Independent States, a colonist aboard the starship Cicada, and a debt slave to the Alterra Corporation. I am with my friends Rauli Isotalo, Udal Singh and Melaku Sansar. Of all the things I have seen and done...this thing is the most important.

Mantis: I leave this marker as testament that we discovered the remnants of an alien civilization on this world we call Proteus. Let our names be remembered by the workers and the pioneers.

Mantis: The computers call these aliens 'Axum'. Something has happened to their civilization...the survivors guard these ruins, but they have been reduced to scavengers: Tailings.

Mantis: I think maybe the virus did it. Seized control of them and drove them mad, as it drives us mad. But I do not know. All I know is that I am here, with my friends, looking on the work of alien minds.

entries.title: MANTIS: Getting through it
entries.body:
Mantis: We could just blast through.

Singh: Yeah, and if we're lucky, Nahema kills us and fills our biobeds with concrete before the Tailings get to us. This is their history. We can't blow it up!

Zip: They do take good care of the exterior...what if the electrical system still works? We could hook up our own generator.

Mantis: Unless you can find me a wiring diagram we'd fry the whole system. You ever see a metal fire underwater?

Zip: What if there ARE wiring diagrams? Instructions right in front of us? And we just can't see them?

Mantis: Did your hot springs tell you about this?

Singh: Actually...she might be right. The Axum see with a combination of sonar and polarized light. Some of their writing might be invisible to us. We'd need to modify our scanners to look for it. Or our eyes.

Zip: Ha! 

Mantis: All right. Let's ask Sophie if we can try out her super scanner. PDA, activate the base camp waypoint. Singh, drop a recording here in case we get reset.

entries.title: Masefield Syndrome
entries.body:
Twinkle: Hey...

Ganzo: Yeah.

Twinkle: Is the apartment sized squid outside keeping you up too?

Ganzo: It's talking to me.

Twinkle: What?

Ganzo: It's saying...come into me and we will dwell in wonder and glory forever. Ow!

Twinkle: Sorry. I thought you were...the Masefield.

Ganzo: I'm joking. I don't hear much when I'm out of the water.

Twinkle: I keep imagining those tentacles reaching up through the moonpool...

Ganzo: ...through the doors and the bulkheads...

Twinkle: ...grabbing us and taking us...

Ganzo: ...to dwell in wonder and glory...

Twinkle: ...the names of its two very hungry children...

Ganzo: [laughter]

Ganzo: Those things killed my wife once.

Twinkle: It's okay. We're trauma bonding.

Ganzo: Sometimes the call feels like this...this yearning to be close. I can see why the Jubilee think the planet wants to help us. It's strong.

Twinkle: Look, as an astronomer, let me tell you that nothing good ever comes from attraction. It's unstable.

Ganzo: What?

Twinkle: If planets just went where they were attracted they'd fall into the sun. If stars went where they were pulled they'd fall into the black hole at the middle of the galaxy. It's the resistance that makes the universe work. The balance between being pulled and trying to get away. When something draws you in, you say: "no, wait. Not yet." Whatever Masefield is, it's not something you're just supposed to obey. Or everything in this ocean would just go and die. Like back in the hot cave.

Ganzo: See, this is why you're not married.

PDA: [loud call]

Twinkle: What was that?

entries.title: NAHEMA: Why
entries.body:
Nahema: Sophie. I'm sorry I destroyed your colony. Please listen: I did it because you couldn't. Because you're a good leader and you never give up on the mission.

Nahema: And I know where this mission will end.

Nahema: NoA is going to start printing duplicates.

Nahema: You, and me, and Zip, and Mantis, and Mogami's kids and Thing One and Thing Two and all the rest. Every morning the biobeds will print ten thousand of us,. Every day we'll maximize our KPIs and OKRs and NoA's utility function will say: these people are happy and healthy, mission complete. Humanity is thriving.

Nahema: Then in the evening we'll all climb into the beds and NoA disintegrates and resets us. A perfect day. Forever. No more mutinies. No more Masefield. No aging, no retirement, no waste. Just peak performance.

Nahema: But NoA couldn't do that on its own. NoA would need human help to bypass its restrictions.

Nahema: And you'd help it, Sophie. I know you would. If it was the only choice left — you'd do whatever it takes to keep your crew alive.

Nahema: So I had to stop you.

entries.title: Powerplant is Dead
entries.body:
Unknown: The powerplant is dead. Spies set fires in its parts. Relatives say the Viceroys did this. Why? Our claws hurt from work. We cannot fix it. We cannot become a molt which can fix it. Sound says this is happening everywhere. The message cables are cut. Why? Xanadu has gone to war against itself.

Unknown: It is hot without the pumps.

entries.title: SINGH: Axum 1
entries.body:
Singh: Field notes, Udal Singh, observing the Axum Tailings in the Karakorum ruins. My reprint site is base camp near the observatory.

Singh: This time they killed me right away. They really don't like us coming into the ruins. They are obviously capable of communication and social behavior. Why are they so damn hostile?

Singh: According to Nahema's research we are the only life on this world not infected by the Proteavirus. Maybe the virus provokes violence against the uninfected? I don't know. It feels too convenient. "The natives hate us because they're sick!" More likely they have good reason to distrust humans.

Singh: And they know we come back when they kill us. So maybe they are communicating. Maybe they're trying to teach us: you cross this line, you die.

entries.title: SINGH: Axum 2
entries.body:
Singh: Field notes, Udal Singh.

Singh: The Karakorum ruins imply a civilization that developed to the same level as pre-Expansion Earth. They had electrical power, industrial metals, digital computers, orbital spaceflight, the whole suite. But they arrived to the same place along a different path.

Singh: They got electricity FIRST. I think they — hang on a minute, I've got to hide — I think the Axum themselves can sense electricity. Like sharks. And they figured out, hey, these long bacterial fibers — these things carry an electrical current. Then some clever Axum wrapped those fibers up into a generator. And once you've got a generator you can make your own electricity and start pulling metal from the seawater. Farming the sea to make tools. All without one spark of fire.

Singh: Imagine it. Undersea cities as old as Troy or Harappa, but with telegraph lines and electric lighting. Thousands of years before the first flame. To us, electricity means modernity. To the Axum...it's as old as bronze.

entries.title: SINGH: Axum 3
entries.body:
PDA: Field Notes, Udal Singh. Think I'm about to die again.

Singh: Listening to that thing pick through the wreckage of my Tadpole. I think it's having fun.

Singh: Humanity exterminated most land-based megafauna before we scratched our first letters into clay. But the Axum never cleared their oceans. They made it all the way to digital computers while coexisting with predators that could eat them like cocktail shrimp.

Singh: Why? Is it something about the ocean? Just too big and deep?

Singh: Or did the Axum have a different relationship with nature? I can already hear you sneering, Iso. "They're lobster aliens, not elves. Don't turn them into a parable."

Singh: Imagine you evolved in an ocean full of leviathans like Grabby out there. Hundreds of generations with those tentacles strangling your nightmares. Then one day you invent the explosive-tipped harpoon and you can fight back. Wouldn't you go out and kill them all? Like we killed the sabertooth tiger and the...I don't know, the automobile?

Singh: So why didn't the Axum do it? Why is Grabby still here to pull my Tadpole to pieces?

entries.title: SINGH: How to see the unseen
entries.body:
Iso: Okay...the screen's on but it's just noise.

Singh: It's noise to us. To Axum, it's a polarized light display.

Iso: How do I read polarized light? 

Singh: Well, either we fabricate some kind of goggles, or...

Iso: Or we find one of Anita's angel combs. And change our eyes.

Singh: Do you think she's on to something?

Iso: I don't think. I just do things.

Singh: PDA, print this to quartz.

Iso: What are *you* thinking?

Singh: Leaving myself clues. In case—you know.

entries.title: SOPHIE: Depth module lost
entries.body:
Mantis: Sorry, boss, what's going on?

Sophie: Wait one. Ruby — it's Butcher. Are you with your Tadpole?

Ruby: Right outside, boss. What's up?

Sophie: Go direct southeast from the research base, about 500 meters, until you find the Cicada wreckage on the tall mesa. Flash your lights and make some noise. Ram it if you have to.

Ruby: Copy. Steer southeast, 500 meters, same depth. Can I ask why?

Sophie: Tuba, Singh and Mel just printed back here. They were testing the upgraded scanner and resonator when Axum Tailings attacked them. Killed them and took Iso. He's still alive. I've tracked his blackbox to the Cicada wreck nearby.

Ruby: Uh...copy. Can't we just wait for them to finish him off?

Sophie: He's got Nahema's super scanner. She'll murder me if I lose it. Try to scare the Tailings off. I'll get a team out there to recover his effects.

Ruby: Boss, I've picked up a couple tails. Looks like — two adult needlers — need to evade —

Mantis: Boss, her Tadpole has our only depth module.

Sophie: Oh, Christ. [muttering] Come on, Ruby...PDA, log my conduct here for kurultai review.

entries.title: SOPHIE: In pursuit of Nahema
entries.body:
Sophie: To any survivors of the Coral Gardens Habitat —

Sophie: I am pursuing Nahema towards the World Tree. She wants to eradicate humanity on Proteus, and she'll destroy the regional ecosystem to do it. I believe she's going to poison the Tree.

Sophie: Do not believe anything she tells you. She is determined to kill us all.

Sophie: I am leaving supplies for anyone who comes after me. If you lose my trail, assume that I failed. Find Nahema. Kill the tree.

Sophie: Good luck. Butcher out.

entries.title: TUBA: New recipe
entries.body:
Tuba: Do you think they were like us?

Singh: No.

Tuba: But look at this. It's a house. They lived in houses, they went to jobs, they ate food. How different could they be?

Singh: Nahema thinks they evolved with the Masefield syndrome. The way we evolved with...day and night, or family.

Tuba: So what? They were all crazy about the tree?

Singh: Well, Sophie thinks Masefield killed them all. And I don't know who's right.

Tuba: Maybe their cooks went on strike and they starved to death. Hey, do you think they tasted good?

Singh: Tuba!

Tuba: What? Don't you like shrimp? PDA, open recipe book.

Singh: TUBA!

entries.title: Turbine Repair Plan
entries.body:
Nahema: Why do you need *me* to fix this?

Mantis: So. The scanner doesn't work. The crystal structure of the turbine blades depends on the growth of bacterial fiber. The scanners see it but don't understand it. No scan, no blueprint, no repairs.

Nahema: Oh.

Mantis: Oh?

Nahema: This is actually an interesting problem. Let me write this down.

Mantis: Isn't it? I imagine they cold-forged these blades from some kind of metal clabber...

Nahema: ...yeah. Okay. We're going to have to modify the scanner. Do you have a mod station at base camp?

Mantis: I told Zip to set it up, but she's still...

Nahema: Yeah. We should talk about that.

Mantis: Someone destroyed this place on purpose. We should talk about that too.

entries.title: TWINKLE: Tailings
entries.body:
Twinkle: [breathing hard] Holy hell.

Ganzo: [breathing hard] Yeah. Damn. Damn, we made it!

Twinkle: When those things —

Ganzo: Tailings.

Twinkle: When the Tailings came after us all I could think was: we aren't going to reprint. This is it. I finally get to go outside and the first thing I do is die on a spear.

Ganzo: Let's sort this cache into 'take' and 'bury'. Why didn't Sophie let you outside?

Twinkle: Why didn't I ever do any work, you mean?

Ganzo: Hey. You always seemed cool. I just never saw you...do anything.

Twinkle: I'm keeping something secret. If I ever got sick and reset, Sophie wasn't sure she could convince me to keep it secret again. So...I had to stay safe.

Ganzo: Ruby gets killed all the time. Really freaks me out.

Twinkle: Yeah...she asked me to listen to a recording once.

Ganzo: Thanks for tricking me into leaving her.

Twinkle: What?

Ganzo: I mean it. If we're all marked 'do not reprint', so is she. She didn't reprint. I would've gone looking for her and...

Twinkle: The Masefield would've got you.

Ganzo: I can hear the tree, Twinkle. It wants me to go into the sea and...feed myself to it.

Twinkle: If you do that you'll never see Ruby again.

Ganzo: Yeah. I know. Anyway, we can't stay here. Tailings don't like the open plains. We can build there and start scanning for the Jubilee.

entries.title: What were they eating?
entries.body:
Twinkle: Ganzo, I don't think this is the Jubilee.

Ganzo: They're us.

Twinkle: That's me. I guess NoA wants an astronomer in the mix.

Ganzo: You figure there's a sleep bay nearby?

Twinkle: Yeah. Same as our group. It printed out some colonists and told them to make a go at it. But these poor people...

Ganzo: They don't have any adaptations. Look at you, your legs are like sticks. You're starving.

Twinkle: They can't digest the food. No angel comb to donate the enzymes. I don't see any growbeds...what are they...oh my God. Ganzo, let's go.

Ganzo: Wait. Wait. We could ask for help. Maybe they've got communications, maybe they've heard from the ship.

Twinkle: Look at the biobeds, Ganzo! Look at that!

Ganzo: Yeah. I see it. It won't work long term, though. The biobeds need feedstock like anything else...why waste it making food? It'd make more sense to reprint yourself every day...unless the point was to test how long you could live on a diet of biobed meat...oh, man.

Twinkle: You are remarkably calm about this.

Ganzo: A lot of animals eat their young under stress.

entries.title: ZIP: Met my old friend
entries.body:
Nahema: What was in those giant tanks?

Zip: Huh? Oh. Um—probably methane. Natural gas.

Nahema: I thought this was a hydroelectric plant.

Zip: Originally, yeah, but the old Axum added a methane burner about...um...hang on, let me check my databank.

Nahema: It's okay. Hey - really. It's okay. Where's Mantis?

Zip: He's up there, on the broken turbine. He thinks you can help him fix it.

Nahema: Up that big arch? All right.

Zip: So...before I got reset...we were friends?

Nahema: Yeah. We were.

Zip: Is it okay if I write that down?

entries.title: PowerPlantConsole OnButtonPressIfNoAdaptation FeedbackDIalog
entries.body: PDA: These screens glow with polarized light incomprehensible to human eyes. Recommend adaptation.

entries.title: Observatory ClimateLab Message PDA Line 01
entries.body: PDA: Architects: we beg your aid.

entries.title: Observatory ClimateLab Message PDA Line 02
entries.body: PDA: The World Trees are dying. Their angels cry out to us.

entries.title: Observatory ClimateLab Message PDA Line 03
entries.body: PDA: The Trees’ roots feed the world, and the world feeds the Trees.

entries.title: Observatory ClimateLab Message PDA Line 04
entries.body: PDA: Each requires other, as we require you.

entries.title: Observatory ClimateLab Message PDA Line 05
entries.body: PDA: But the roots are rotting.

entries.title: Observatory ClimateLab Message PDA Line 06
entries.body: PDA: The nuclear heart dims.

entries.title: Observatory ClimateLab Message PDA Line 07
entries.body: PDA: There is hope.

entries.title: Observatory ClimateLab Message PDA Line 08
entries.body: PDA: Cross the great trench to Xanadu.

entries.title: Cicada Aux. MedBay Report
entries.body:
- [PLACEHOLDER] Medical Log - Cicada- Medbay A

**Timestamp:** [REDACTED]

**Alert:**  Hull Breach Detected. Emergency Life Support Activated. Everything is Fine!

**Colonist Status Records:**

- **Cryo-Pod ID:** 478-B
    - **Name:** Zygimantis “Mantis” Tarvydas
    - **Cryo-Sleep Status:** Stable
    - **Monitoring Notes:** Minor REM disturbances detected. Skeletal regeneration progressing as expected.
- **Cryo-Pod ID:** 921-A
    - **Name:** 吴量海
    - **Cryo-Sleep Status:** Stable but requires close monitoring.
    - **Monitoring Notes:** Cellular regeneration slightly below baseline. Adjusting nanite flow to burn site.
- **Cryo-Pod ID:** 633-C
    - **Name:** Adam Jensen
    - **Cryo-Sleep Status:** Unstable
    - **Monitoring Notes:** Elevated brain activity detected. Possible nightmare scenario. Administering virtual calming agents. Vital signs fluctuating.
    

**Crash Event Log:**

- **[Time]:** Severe turbulence detected. Cryo-pod stabilization increased to maximum.
- **[Time]:** Hull breach in Section 4. Explosive decompression.
- **[Time]:** Emergency bulkheads sealed. Cryo-pod containment field fluctuating.
- **[Time]:** Cryo-Pod 633-C experiencing critical instability. Tachycardia detected.
- **[Time]:** Impact imminent. * [Recording cuts out]*

**Post-Crash Assessment:**

- **Medbay 7:** Structural integrity compromised. Auxiliary power online.
- **Cryo-Pods:** Containment field stabilized.
- **Patient 478-B:** Stable.
- **Patient 921-A:** Stable.
- **Patient 633-C:** Cryo-sleep failure. Patient deceased.

entries.title: MAC chip and
entries.body:
[PLACEHOLDER] - Imagine how much easier our lives would be if that MAC wasn’t a pulverized hunk of junk? Fields recordings, data analysis, navigation charting, survival optiminization…it might even have made me WANT to go inside those caves. But I won’t. I’d rather be eaten by a Marrowbreach. Hell, I’d rather be slowly digested by an army of slugs.

I’ve retrieved the data chip from the MAC though, it was never fully functional but somehow managed to do a light habitability analysis of that side of the planet’s surface, before getting mashed to bits by the atmosphere, blown apart by the impact, and corroded by the water.

HEAD’S UP to our people coming and going to the Habitat - we retrieved and salvaged most of what little remained of the medical hut module of the Cicada. the pieces that this big weird dome thing didn't absorb anyway.  I left some emergency supplies, and will try to keep that place stocked up a little. Don’t expect much though. Will come back at some point to dismantle the last of the bacterial containement tank.

entries.title: Rad Readings
entries.body:
[PLACEHOLDER] - The refinery was out of fuel again last week. Reminder to get more biomass from around to feed that reactor. Ask main camp for a survey, maybe they’ll find new hotspots. Or better, install solar panels too!! 

The Gravity Drive module is one serious pain in the butt to explore. A lot of good salvage there, much needed tech. Voluneteers for another expedition to that humungus pile of electromagnetic junk is welcome and will be paid with a nice bowl of sticky shootroot soup. Yum! Aren’t you excited?
